revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

revision.text: {{神社| |名称 = 清瀧神社| |画像 = |所在地 = [[千葉県]][[浦安市]]堀江四丁目1-5| |位置 = |祭神 = 大綿津見神| |社格 = | |創建 = 建久7年（1196年）| |本殿 = |別名 = 新宿十二社 十二社熊野神社 角筈十二社 など| |例祭 = [[6月15日]]| |神事 = }} 清瀧神社（せいりゅうじんじゃ）は[[千葉県]][[浦安市]]堀江四丁目にある[[神社]]。 ==概要== 本殿は浦安市の指定文化財。また神紋は右三つ巴である。 ==歴史== *[[1196年]]（建久7年） - 創建 *[[1299年]]（永仁元年） - 津波に遭い本殿が倒壊 **更に元禄・享保・寛政の各年間にも津波の被害に遭う *[[1855年]]（安政2年） - 本殿が再築され、狛犬が奉納された *[[1896年]]（明治29年） - 拝殿が建立される *[[1923年]]（大正12年） - 拝殿を修築する *[[1971年]]（昭和46年） - 社務所と玉垣が建築される *[[1982年]]（昭和57年） - 本殿が浦安市有形文化財に指定される ==境内== *本殿（浦安市指定文化財） *拝殿 *社務所 *堀江水準標石（土木学会推奨土木遺産） ==境内社== *八坂神社 *龍神社 *道祖神社 *浅間神社 ==祭事== *例大祭は4年に一度清瀧神社・稲荷神社・豊受神社の三社で行われる *[[1月1日]] - 歳旦祭 *[[2月3日]] - 節分祭 *[[2月11日]] - 建国記念祭 *[[2月17日]] - 祈年祭 *[[6月15日]] - 例祭 *[[6月30日]] - 夏越大祓式 *[[11月23日]] - 新嘗祭 *[[12月31日]] - 年越大祓式 *毎月1日・15日 - 月次祭 [[Category:千葉県の神社|せいりゆうしんしや]] [[Category:浦安市|せいりゆうしんしや]]

{{神社| |名称 = 清瀧神社| |画像 = |所在地 = [[千葉県]][[浦安市]]堀江四丁目1-5| |位置 = |祭神 = 大綿津見神| |社格 = | |創建 = 建久7年（1196年）| |本殿 = |別名 = | |例祭 = [[6月15日]]| |神事 = }} 清瀧神社（せいりゅうじんじゃ）は[[千葉県]][[浦安市]]堀江四丁目にある[[神社]]。 ==概要== 本殿は浦安市の指定文化財。また神紋は右三つ巴である。 ==歴史== *[[1196年]]（建久7年） - 創建 *[[1299年]]（永仁元年） - 津波に遭い本殿が倒壊 **更に元禄・享保・寛政の各年間にも津波の被害に遭う *[[1855年]]（安政2年） - 本殿が再築され、狛犬が奉納された *[[1896年]]（明治29年） - 拝殿が建立される *[[1923年]]（大正12年） - 拝殿を修築する *[[1971年]]（昭和46年） - 社務所と玉垣が建築される *[[1982年]]（昭和57年） - 本殿が浦安市有形文化財に指定される ==境内== *本殿（浦安市指定文化財） *拝殿 *社務所 *堀江水準標石（土木学会推奨土木遺産） ==境内社== *八坂神社 *龍神社 *道祖神社 *浅間神社 ==祭事== *例大祭は4年に一度清瀧神社・稲荷神社・豊受神社の三社で行われる *[[1月1日]] - 歳旦祭 *[[2月3日]] - 節分祭 *[[2月11日]] - 建国記念祭 *[[2月17日]] - 祈年祭 *[[6月15日]] - 例祭 *[[6月30日]] - 夏越大祓式 *[[11月23日]] - 新嘗祭 *[[12月31日]] - 年越大祓式 *毎月1日・15日 - 月次祭 [[Category:千葉県の神社|せいりゆうしんしや]] [[Category:浦安市|せいりゆうしんしや]]

{{神社| |名称 = 清瀧神社| |画像 = |所在地 = [[千葉県]][[浦安市]]堀江四丁目1-5| |位置 = |祭神 = 大綿津見神| |社格 = | |創建 = 建久7年（1196年）| |本殿 = |別名 = | |例祭 = [[6月15日]]| |神事 = }} '''清瀧神社'''（せいりゅうじんじゃ）は[[千葉県]][[浦安市]]堀江四丁目にある[[神社]]。
==概要== 本殿は浦安市の指定文化財。また神紋は右三つ巴である。
==歴史== *[[1196年]]（建久7年） - 創建 *[[1299年]]（永仁元年） - 津波に遭い本殿が倒壊 **更に元禄・享保・寛政の各年間にも津波の被害に遭う *[[1855年]]（安政2年） - 本殿が再築され、狛犬が奉納された *[[1896年]]（明治29年） - 拝殿が建立される *[[1923年]]（大正12年） - 拝殿を修築する *[[1971年]]（昭和46年） - 社務所と玉垣が建築される *[[1982年]]（昭和57年） - 本殿が浦安市有形文化財に指定される
==境内== *本殿（浦安市指定文化財） *拝殿 *社務所 *堀江水準標石（土木学会推奨土木遺産）
==境内社== *八坂神社 *龍神社 *道祖神社 *浅間神社
==祭事== *例大祭は4年に一度清瀧神社・稲荷神社・豊受神社の三社で行われる *[[1月1日]] - 歳旦祭 *[[2月3日]] - 節分祭 *[[2月11日]] - 建国記念祭 *[[2月17日]] - 祈年祭 *[[6月15日]] - 例祭 *[[6月30日]] - 夏越大祓式 *[[11月23日]] - 新嘗祭 *[[12月31日]] - 年越大祓式 *毎月1日・15日 - 月次祭
{{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=千葉県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:せいりゆうしんしや}} [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:浦安市]]

revision.comment: 「[[清瀧神社]]」を「[[清瀧神社 (浦安市)]]」へ移動: 曖昧さ回避を作成するため
revision.text:
{{神社| |名称 = 清瀧神社| |画像 = |所在地 = [[千葉県]][[浦安市]]堀江四丁目1-5| |位置 = |祭神 = 大綿津見神| |社格 = | |創建 = 建久7年（1196年）| |本殿 = |別名 = | |例祭 = [[6月15日]]| |神事 = }} '''清瀧神社'''（せいりゅうじんじゃ）は[[千葉県]][[浦安市]]堀江四丁目にある[[神社]]。
==概要== 本殿は浦安市の指定文化財。また神紋は右三つ巴である。

==境内== *本殿（浦安市指定文化財） *拝殿 *社務所 *堀江水準標石（土木学会推奨土木遺産）
==境内社== *八坂神社 *龍神社 *道祖神社 *浅間神社
==祭事== *例大祭は4年に一度清瀧神社・稲荷神社・豊受神社の三社で行われる *[[1月1日]] - 歳旦祭 *[[2月3日]] - 節分祭 *[[2月11日]] - 建国記念祭 *[[2月17日]] - 祈年祭 *[[6月15日]] - 例祭 *[[6月30日]] - 夏越大祓式 *[[11月23日]] - 新嘗祭 *[[12月31日]] - 年越大祓式 *毎月1日・15日 - 月次祭

{{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:せいりゆうしんしや}} [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:浦安市の歴史]]

{{神社| |名称 = 清瀧神社| |画像 = [[File:Seiryu jinja urayasu haiden.JPG|300px|拝殿]]| |所在地 = [[千葉県]][[浦安市]]堀江四丁目1-5| |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|43.81|N|139|53|28.27|E|scale:5000}} |祭神 = 大綿津見神| |社格 = | |創建 = 建久7年（1196年）| |本殿 = |別名 = | |例祭 = [[6月15日]]| |神事 = }} [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 01.jpg|thumb|2012年6月に新造された一之鳥居]] '''清瀧神社'''（せいりゅうじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[浦安市]]堀江にある[[神社]]。
== 概要 == [[祭神]]は[[ワタツミ|大綿津見神]]（海の神）。本殿は[[上総国]]の[[ケヤキ]]の大木を使用し[[1855年]]（[[安政]]2年）9月に造られたもので、浦安市の指定文化財となっている。また、[[神社建築|建築様式]]は木造三間社[[流造|流造り]]で、[[神紋]]は右三つ[[巴]]である。
== 歴史 == * [[1196年]]（[[建久]]7年） - この年の創建と伝えられる。 * [[1299年]]（[[永仁]]元年） - 津波に遭い本殿が倒壊。 ** 更に元禄・享保・寛政の各年間にも津波の被害に遭う。 * [[1855年]]（[[安政]]2年） - 本殿が再築され、狛犬が奉納された。 * [[1896年]]（[[明治]]29年） - 拝殿が建立される。 * [[1923年]]（[[大正]]12年） - 拝殿を修築する。 * [[1971年]]（[[昭和]]46年） - 社務所と玉垣が建築される。 * [[1982年]]（昭和57年） - 本殿が浦安市有形文化財に指定される。 * [[2012年]]（[[平成]]24年） - 一之鳥居が建替えにより新造される<ref>[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/seiryuujinnjya/seibi-s-1.html 清瀧神社一之鳥居竣工清祓式 平成24年6月2日]（清瀧神社公式サイト内）</ref>。
== 境内 == * 本殿（浦安市指定文化財） * 拝殿 * 社務所 * 堀江水準標石（[[土木学会選奨土木遺産]]）
== 境内社 == [[境内]]には以下の[[摂末社|末社]]がある。 * [[八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|八坂神社]] * [[龍神社]] * [[道祖神|道祖]]神社 * [[浅間神社]]
== 祭事 == [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 02.jpg|thumb|夏越大祓式における[[茅の輪]]]] ; 年間行事 * [[1月1日]] - [[歳旦祭]] * [[2月3日]] - [[節分]]祭 * [[2月11日]] - 建国記念祭 * [[2月17日]] - [[祈年祭]] * [[6月15日]] - [[例祭]] ** 4年に一度、清瀧神社・稲荷神社・豊受神社の三社で例大祭が行われる<ref>[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/urayasusannjya/ 浦安三社]</ref>。 * [[6月30日]] - 夏越[[大祓]]式 * [[11月23日]] - [[新嘗祭]] * [[12月31日]] - 年越大祓式 ; 月次祭 * 毎月1日・15日 - 月次祭
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Seiryu jinja (urayasu)}} * [http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/seiryuujinnjya/ 千葉県浦安三社 清瀧神社] * [http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/kanko/716/ ちばの観光まるごと紹介 清瀧神社（浦安市）]
{{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:せいりゆうしんしや}} [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:浦安市の歴史]]

{{神社 |名称 = 清瀧神社 |画像 = [[File:Seiryu jinja urayasu haiden.JPG|300px|拝殿]] |所在地 = [[千葉県]][[浦安市]]堀江四丁目1-5 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|43.81|N|139|53|28.27|E|scale:5000}} |祭神 = 大綿津見神 |社格 = |創建 = 建久7年（1196年） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = [[6月15日]] |神事 = }} [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 01.jpg|thumb|2012年6月に新造された一之鳥居]] '''清瀧神社'''（せいりゅうじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[浦安市]]堀江にある[[神社]]。
== 概要 == [[祭神]]は[[ワタツミ|大綿津見神]]（海の神）。本殿は[[上総国]]の[[ケヤキ]]の大木を使用し[[1855年]]（[[安政]]2年）9月に造られたもので、浦安市の指定文化財となっている。また、[[神社建築|建築様式]]は木造三間社[[流造|流造り]]で、[[神紋]]は右三つ[[巴]]である。
== 歴史 == * [[1196年]]（[[建久]]7年） - この年の創建と伝えられる。 * [[1299年]]（[[永仁]]元年） - 津波に遭い本殿が倒壊。 ** 更に元禄・享保・寛政の各年間にも津波の被害に遭う。 * [[1855年]]（[[安政]]2年） - 本殿が再築され、狛犬が奉納された。 * [[1896年]]（[[明治]]29年） - 拝殿が建立される。 * [[1923年]]（[[大正]]12年） - 拝殿を修築する。 * [[1971年]]（[[昭和]]46年） - 社務所と玉垣が建築される。 * [[1982年]]（昭和57年） - 本殿が浦安市有形文化財に指定される。 * [[2012年]]（[[平成]]24年） - 一之[[鳥居]]が建替えにより新造される<ref>[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/seiryuujinnjya/seibi-s-1.html 清瀧神社一之鳥居竣工清祓式 平成24年6月2日]（清瀧神社公式サイト内）</ref>。
== 境内 == * 本殿（浦安市指定文化財） * 拝殿 * 社務所 * 堀江水準標石（[[土木学会選奨土木遺産]]）
== 境内社 == [[境内]]には以下の[[摂末社|末社]]がある。
* [[八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|八坂神社]] * [[龍神社]] * [[道祖神|道祖]]神社 * [[浅間神社]]
== 祭事 == [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 02.jpg|thumb|夏越大祓式における[[茅の輪]]]] ; 年間行事 * [[1月1日]] - [[歳旦祭]] * [[2月3日]] - [[節分]]祭 * [[2月11日]] - 建国記念祭 * [[2月17日]] - [[祈年祭]] * [[6月15日]] - [[例祭]] ** 4年に一度、清瀧神社・稲荷神社・豊受神社の三社で例大祭が行われる<ref>[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/urayasusannjya/ 浦安三社]</ref>。 * [[6月30日]] - 夏越[[大祓]]式 * [[11月23日]] - [[新嘗祭]] * [[12月31日]] - 年越大祓式 ; 月次祭 * 毎月1日・15日 - 月次祭
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[せいりゅう (潜水艦)]] 同艦の御守を頒布している。
== 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Seiryu jinja (urayasu)}} * [http://www.3ja.info/seiryujinja/ 千葉県浦安三社 清瀧神社] * [http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/kanko/716/ ちばの観光まるごと紹介 清瀧神社（浦安市）]
{{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:せいりゆうしんしや}} [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:浦安市の歴史]] [[Category:浦安市の建築物]]

{{神社 |名称 = 清瀧神社 |画像 = [[File:Seiryu jinja urayasu haiden.JPG|300px|拝殿]] |所在地 = [[千葉県]][[浦安市]]堀江四丁目1-5 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|43.81|N|139|53|28.27|E|scale:5000}} |祭神 = 大綿津見神 |社格 = |創建 = 建久7年（1196年） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = [[6月15日]] |神事 = }} [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 01.jpg|thumb|2012年6月に新造された一之鳥居]] '''清瀧神社'''（せいりゅうじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[浦安市]]堀江にある[[神社]]。
== 概要 == [[祭神]]は[[ワタツミ|大綿津見神]]（海の神）。本殿は[[上総国]]の[[ケヤキ]]の大木を使用し[[1855年]]（[[安政]]2年）9月に造られたもので、浦安市の指定文化財となっている。また、[[神社建築|建築様式]]は木造三間社[[流造|流造り]]で、[[神紋]]は右三つ[[巴]]である。
== 歴史 == * [[1196年]]（[[建久]]7年） - この年の創建と伝えられる。 * [[1299年]]（[[永仁]]元年） - 津波に遭い本殿が倒壊。 ** 更に元禄・享保・寛政の各年間にも津波の被害に遭う。 * [[1855年]]（[[安政]]2年） - 本殿が再築され、狛犬が奉納された。 * [[1896年]]（[[明治]]29年） - 拝殿が建立される。 * [[1923年]]（[[大正]]12年） - 拝殿を修築する。 * [[1971年]]（[[昭和]]46年） - 社務所と玉垣が建築される。 * [[1982年]]（昭和57年） - 本殿が浦安市有形文化財に指定される。 * [[2012年]]（[[平成]]24年） - 一之[[鳥居]]が建替えにより新造される<ref>[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/seiryuujinnjya/seibi-s-1.html 清瀧神社一之鳥居竣工清祓式 平成24年6月2日]（清瀧神社公式サイト内）</ref>。
== 境内 == * 本殿（浦安市指定文化財） * 拝殿 * 社務所 * 堀江水準標石（[[土木学会選奨土木遺産]]） *: 江戸川堀江水位尺（[[量水標]]）の零位の基準となっていたもので、千葉県[[銚子市]]の飯沼観音（圓福寺）内に設置された飯沼水準原標石とともに日本最古の水準標石である<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.ktr.mlit.go.jp/edogawa/edogawa00229.html|title=堀江水準標石|work=|publisher=江戸川河川事務所|date=|accessdate=2022-12-19}}</ref>。

* [[八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|八坂神社]] * [[龍神社]] * [[道祖神|道祖]]神社 * [[浅間神社]]

revision.text:
'''Kiyotaki Shrine''' (清瀧神社 / 清滝神社) refers to Shinto shrines located across Japan.
* {{ill|Kiyotaki Shrine (Nikkō City)|ja|清瀧神社 (日光市)}} - Located in {{ill|Nikkō City|ja|日光市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}. * {{ill|Kiyotaki Shrine (Urayasu City)|ja|清瀧神社 (浦安市)}} - Located in {{ill|Urayasu City|ja|浦安市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. * {{ill|Kiyotaki Shrine (Kanazawa City)|ja|清瀧神社 (金沢市)}} - Located in {{ill|Kanazawa City|ja|金沢市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}. * {{ill|Kiyotaki Shrine (Ōno City)|ja|清瀧神社 (大野市)}} - Located in {{ill|Ōno City|ja|大野市}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}}. * {{ill|Kiyotaki Shrine (Ōno City Tomoe)|ja|清滝神社 (大野市友江)}} - Located in Tomoe, Ōno City, Fukui Prefecture. * {{ill|Kiyotaki Shrine (Ōno City Nakasaba)|ja|清滝神社 (大野市中狭)}} - Located in Nakasaba, Ōno City, Fukui Prefecture.
{{Disambiguation}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:Kiyotaki Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines with identical names]]
[[Category:Link incomplete]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[category:Unsorted link categories]] [[ja:清瀧神社]]

'''Kiyotaki Shrine''' (清瀧神社 / 清滝神社) refers to Shinto shrines located across Japan.
* {{ill|Kiyotaki Shrine (Nikkō City)|ja|清瀧神社 (日光市)}} - Located in {{ill|Nikkō City|ja|日光市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}. * {{ill|Kiyotaki Shrine (Urayasu City)|ja|清瀧神社 (浦安市)}} - Located in {{ill|Urayasu City|ja|浦安市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. * {{ill|Kiyotaki Shrine (Kanazawa City)|ja|清瀧神社 (金沢市)}} - Located in {{ill|Kanazawa City|ja|金沢市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}. * {{ill|Kiyotaki Shrine (Ōno City)|ja|清瀧神社 (大野市)}} - Located in {{ill|Ōno City|ja|大野市}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}}. * {{ill|Kiyotaki Shrine (Ōno City Tomoe)|ja|清滝神社 (大野市友江)}} - Located in Tomoe, Ōno City, Fukui Prefecture. * {{ill|Kiyotaki Shrine (Ōno City Nakasaba)|ja|清滝神社 (大野市中狭)}} - Located in Nakasaba, Ōno City, Fukui Prefecture.

{{Shrine | Name = Seiryū Shrine | Image = [[File:Seiryu jinja urayasu haiden.JPG|250px|Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|39|43.8|N|139|53|28.2|E}}}} | Location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Urayasu|ja|浦安市}}, Horie 4-1-5 | Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|43.8|N|139|53|28.2|E|scale:5000}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ōwatatsumi|ja|大綿津見神}} | Shrine Rank = | Founded = {{ill|Kenkyū|ja|建久}} 7 (1196) | Main Hall = | Alternate Name = | Annual Festival = June 15 | Rituals = }} [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 01.jpg|thumb|230px|The first torii, rebuilt in June 2012]] '''Seiryū Shrine''' (清瀧神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Urayasu|ja|浦安市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Horie district. == Overview == The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|祭神}} of the shrine is {{ill|Ōwatatsumi|ja|大綿津見神}}, the god of the sea. The main hall, constructed in {{ill|Ansei|ja|安政}} 2 (1855), uses wood from large zelkova trees from the {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} and is designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. The architectural style is wooden, three-bay {{ill|nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, and the shrine crest is a right-facing mitsudomoe (triple swirl). == History == * {{ill|1196|ja|1196年}} (Kenkyū 7): The shrine was founded according to tradition. * {{ill|1299|ja|1299年}} (Einin 1): The main hall was destroyed by a tsunami. ** The shrine also suffered tsunami damage during the Genroku, Kyōhō, and Kansei eras. * 1855 (Ansei 2): The main hall was rebuilt, and guardian lion-dog statues (komainu) were donated. * 1896 (Meiji 29): The worship hall (haiden) was constructed. * 1923 (Taishō 12): The worship hall was renovated. * 1971 (Shōwa 46): A shrine office and stone fence (tamagaki) were constructed. * 1982 (Shōwa 57): The main hall was designated a tangible cultural property by Urayasu City. * 2012 (Heisei 24): The first {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} was rebuilt.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/seiryuujinnjya/seibi-s-1.html Seiryū Shrine First Torii Rebuilding Ceremony, June 2, 2012] (Seiryū Shrine Official Website) == Grounds == * Main Hall - Designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Horie Level Reference Stone - Designated as a {{ill|Civil Engineering Heritage|ja|土木学会選奨土木遺産}}. It served as the zero level reference for the Edo River water gauge and is one of the oldest level reference stones in Japan, alongside the Iinuma Level Reference Stone in {{ill|Chōshi|ja|銚子市}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.ktr.mlit.go.jp/edogawa/edogawa00229.html|title=Horie Level Reference Stone|publisher=Edo River Office|accessdate=2022-12-19}} == Auxiliary Shrines == The grounds contain the following subordinate shrines: * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} * {{ill|Ryūjin Shrine|ja|龍神社}} * {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}} Shrine * {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} == Annual Festivals == [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 02.jpg|thumb|Summer purification ritual (chinowa kuguri)]] ; Annual Events * January 1: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * February 3: {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭}} * February 11: Foundation Day Festival * February 17: {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} * June 15: Annual Festival ** Every four years, the grand festival (例大祭) is celebrated jointly by Seiryū Shrine, {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}, and {{ill|Toyouke Shrine|ja|豊受神社}}.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/urayasusannjya/ Urayasu Three Shrines] * June 30: {{ill|Nagoshi-no-Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓}} * November 23: {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 31: Year-end Ōharae ; Monthly Festivals * 1st and 15th of each month: Monthly Festival (Tsukinami-sai) == Related Items == * The submarine {{ill|Seiryū|ja|せいりゅう (潜水艦)}} distributes amulets blessed at the shrine. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Seiryū-class submarine|ja|清瀧}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Seiryu jinja (urayasu)}} * [http://www.3ja.info/seiryujinja/ Urayasu Three Shrines - Seiryū Shrine] * [http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/kanko/716/ Chiba Prefecture Tourism - Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Urayasu City Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Urayasu]] [[Category:Link incomplete]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[category:Unsorted link categories]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[ja:%E6%B8%85%E7%80%A7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E6%B5%A6%E5%AE%89%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:清瀧神社]]

Annual Events * January 1: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * February 3: {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭}} * February 11: Foundation Day Festival * February 17: {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} * June 15: Annual Festival ** Every four years, the grand festival (例大祭) is celebrated jointly by Seiryū Shrine, {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}, and {{ill|Toyouke Shrine|ja|豊受神社}}.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/urayasusannjya/ Urayasu Three Shrines] * June 30: {{ill|Nagoshi-no-Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓}} * November 23: {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 31: Year-end Ōharae ; {{Shrine | Name = Seiryū Shrine | Image = [[File:Seiryu jinja urayasu haiden.JPG|250px|Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|39|43.8|N|139|53|28.2|E}}}} | Location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Urayasu|ja|浦安市}}, Horie 4-1-5 | Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|43.8|N|139|53|28.2|E|scale:5000}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ōwatatsumi|ja|大綿津見神}} | Shrine Rank = | Founded = {{ill|Kenkyū|ja|建久}} 7 (1196) | Main Hall = | Alternate Name = | Annual Festival = June 15 | Rituals = }} [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 01.jpg|thumb|230px|The first torii, rebuilt in June 2012]] '''Seiryū Shrine''' (清瀧神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Urayasu|ja|浦安市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Horie district. == Overview == The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|祭神}} of the shrine is [[Watatsumi]], the god of the sea. The main hall, constructed in {{ill|Ansei|ja|安政}} 2 (1855), uses wood from large zelkova trees from the {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} and is designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. The architectural style is wooden, three-bay {{ill|nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, and the shrine crest is a right-facing mitsudomoe (triple swirl). == History == * {{ill|1196|ja|1196年}} (Kenkyū 7): The shrine was founded according to tradition. * {{ill|1299|ja|1299年}} (Einin 1): The main hall was destroyed by a tsunami. ** The shrine also suffered tsunami damage during the Genroku, Kyōhō, and Kansei eras. * 1855 (Ansei 2): The main hall was rebuilt, and guardian lion-dog statues (komainu) were donated. * 1896 (Meiji 29): The worship hall (haiden) was constructed. * 1923 (Taishō 12): The worship hall was renovated. * 1971 (Shōwa 46): A shrine office and stone fence (tamagaki) were constructed. * 1982 (Shōwa 57): The main hall was designated a tangible cultural property by Urayasu City. * 2012 (Heisei 24): The first {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} was rebuilt.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/seiryuujinnjya/seibi-s-1.html Seiryū Shrine First Torii Rebuilding Ceremony, June 2, 2012] (Seiryū Shrine Official Website) == Grounds == * Main Hall - Designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Horie Level Reference Stone - Designated as a {{ill|Civil Engineering Heritage|ja|土木学会選奨土木遺産}}. It served as the zero level reference for the Edo River water gauge and is one of the oldest level reference stones in Japan, alongside the Iinuma Level Reference Stone in {{ill|Chōshi|ja|銚子市}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.ktr.mlit.go.jp/edogawa/edogawa00229.html|title=Horie Level Reference Stone|publisher=Edo River Office|accessdate=2022-12-19}} == Auxiliary Shrines == The grounds contain the following subordinate shrines: * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} * {{ill|Ryūjin Shrine|ja|龍神社}} * {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}} Shrine * {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} == Annual Festivals == [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 02.jpg|thumb|Summer purification ritual (chinowa kuguri)]] ; Annual Events * January 1: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * February 3: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分祭|Setsubun Festival=Setsubun Festival|lt=Setsubun Festival}} * February 11: Foundation Day Festival * February 17: {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} * June 15: Annual Festival ** Every four years, the grand festival (例大祭) is celebrated jointly by Seiryū Shrine, {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}, and {{ill|Toyouke Shrine|ja|豊受神社}}.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/urayasusannjya/ Urayasu Three Shrines] * June 30: {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|ja|夏越大祓|lt=Nagoshi-no-Ōharae}} * November 23: {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 31: Year-end Ōharae ; Monthly Festivals * 1st and 15th of each month: Monthly Festival (Tsukinami-sai) == Related Items == * The submarine {{ill|Seiryū|ja|せいりゅう (潜水艦)}} distributes amulets blessed at the shrine. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Seiryū-class submarine|ja|清瀧}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Seiryu jinja (urayasu)}} * [http://www.3ja.info/seiryujinja/ Urayasu Three Shrines - Seiryū Shrine] * [http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/kanko/716/ Chiba Prefecture Tourism - Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Urayasu City Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Urayasu]] [[Category:Link incomplete]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Images]] [[ja:%E6%B8%85%E7%80%A7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E6%B5%A6%E5%AE%89%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:清瀧神社]]

The architectural style is wooden, three-bay {{ill|nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, and the shrine crest is a right-facing mitsudomoe (triple swirl). == History == * {{ill|1196|ja|1196年}} (Kenkyū 7): The shrine was founded according to tradition. * {{ill|1299|ja|1299年}} (Einin 1): The main hall was destroyed by a tsunami. ** The shrine also suffered tsunami damage during the Genroku, Kyōhō, and Kansei eras. * 1855 (Ansei 2): The main hall was rebuilt, and guardian lion-dog statues (komainu) were donated. * 1896 (Meiji 29): The worship hall (haiden) was constructed. * 1923 (Taishō 12): The worship hall was renovated. * 1971 (Shōwa 46): A shrine office and stone fence (tamagaki) were constructed. * 1982 (Shōwa 57): The main hall was designated a tangible cultural property by Urayasu City. * 2012 (Heisei 24): The first {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} was rebuilt.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/seiryuujinnjya/seibi-s-1.html Seiryū Shrine First Torii Rebuilding Ceremony, June 2, 2012] (Seiryū Shrine Official Website) == Grounds == * Main Hall - Designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Horie Level Reference Stone - Designated as a {{ill|Civil Engineering Heritage|ja|土木学会選奨土木遺産}}. revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Seiryū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Seiryu jinja urayasu haiden.JPG|250px|Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|39|43.8|N|139|53|28.2|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Urayasu|ja|浦安市}}, Horie 4-1-5 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|43.8|N|139|53|28.2|E|scale:5000}} | deity = {{ill|Ōwatatsumi|ja|大綿津見神}} | type = | consecration_year = {{ill|Kenkyū|ja|建久}} 7 (1196) | Main Hall = | Alternate name = | festival = June 15 | Rituals = }} [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 01.jpg|thumb|230px|The first torii, rebuilt in June 2012]] '''Seiryū Shrine''' (清瀧神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Urayasu|ja|浦安市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Horie district. == Overview == The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|祭神}} of the shrine is [[Watatsumi]], the god of the sea. The main hall, constructed in {{ill|Ansei|ja|安政}} 2 (1855), uses wood from large zelkova trees from the {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} and is designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. The architectural style is wooden, three-bay {{ill|nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, and the shrine crest is a right-facing mitsudomoe (triple swirl). == History == * {{ill|1196|ja|1196年}} (Kenkyū 7): The shrine was founded according to tradition. * {{ill|1299|ja|1299年}} (Einin 1): The main hall was destroyed by a tsunami. ** The shrine also suffered tsunami damage during the Genroku, Kyōhō, and Kansei eras. * 1855 (Ansei 2): The main hall was rebuilt, and guardian lion-dog statues (komainu) were donated. * 1896 (Meiji 29): The worship hall (haiden) was constructed. * 1923 (Taishō 12): The worship hall was renovated. * 1971 (Shōwa 46): A shrine office and stone fence (tamagaki) were constructed. * 1982 (Shōwa 57): The main hall was designated a tangible cultural property by Urayasu City. * 2012 (Heisei 24): The first {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} was rebuilt.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/seiryuujinnjya/seibi-s-1.html Seiryū Shrine First Torii Rebuilding Ceremony, June 2, 2012] (Seiryū Shrine Official Website) == Grounds == * Main Hall - Designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Horie Level Reference Stone - Designated as a {{ill|Civil Engineering Heritage|ja|土木学会選奨土木遺産}}. It served as the zero level reference for the Edo River water gauge and is one of the oldest level reference stones in Japan, alongside the Iinuma Level Reference Stone in {{ill|Chōshi|ja|銚子市}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.ktr.mlit.go.jp/edogawa/edogawa00229.html|title=Horie Level Reference Stone|publisher=Edo River Office|accessdate=2022-12-19}} == Auxiliary Shrines == The grounds contain the following subordinate shrines: * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} * {{ill|Ryūjin Shrine|ja|龍神社}} * {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}} Shrine * {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} == festivals == [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 02.jpg|thumb|Summer purification ritual (chinowa kuguri)]] ; Annual Events * January 1: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * February 3: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分祭|Setsubun Festival=Setsubun Festival|lt=Setsubun Festival}} * February 11: Foundation Day Festival * February 17: {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} * June 15: festival ** Every four years, the grand festival (例大祭) is celebrated jointly by Seiryū Shrine, {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}, and {{ill|Toyouke Shrine|ja|豊受神社}}.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/urayasusannjya/ Urayasu Three Shrines] * June 30: {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|ja|夏越大祓|lt=Nagoshi-no-Ōharae}} * November 23: {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 31: Year-end Ōharae ; Monthly Festivals * 1st and 15th of each month: Monthly Festival (Tsukinami-sai) == Related Items == * The submarine [[:en:JS_Seiryū|JS Seiryū]] distributes amulets blessed at the shrine. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Seiryū-class submarine|ja|清瀧}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Seiryu jinja (urayasu)}} * [http://www.3ja.info/seiryujinja/ Urayasu Three Shrines - Seiryū Shrine] * [http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/kanko/716/ Chiba Prefecture Tourism - Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Urayasu City Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Urayasu]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:images]] [[ja:%E6%B8%85%E7%80%A7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E6%B5%A6%E5%AE%89%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:清瀧神社]]

{{Shrine | name = Seiryū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Seiryu jinja urayasu haiden.JPG|250px|Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|39|43.8|N|139|53|28.2|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Urayasu|ja|浦安市}}, Horie 4-1-5 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|43.8|N|139|53|28.2|E|scale:5000}} | deity = {{ill|Ōwatatsumi|ja|大綿津見神}} | type = | consecration_year = {{ill|Kenkyū|ja|建久}} 7 (1196) | Main Hall = | Alternate name = | festival = June 15 | Rituals = }} [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 01.jpg|thumb|230px|The first torii, rebuilt in June 2012]] '''Seiryū Shrine''' (清瀧神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Urayasu|ja|浦安市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Horie district. == Overview == The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|祭神}} of the shrine is [[Watatsumi]], the god of the sea. The main hall, constructed in {{ill|Ansei|ja|安政}} 2 (1855), uses wood from large zelkova trees from the {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} and is designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. The architectural style is wooden, three-bay {{ill|nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, and the shrine crest is a right-facing mitsudomoe (triple swirl). == History == * {{ill|1196|ja|1196年}} (Kenkyū 7): The shrine was founded according to tradition. * {{ill|1299|ja|1299年}} (Einin 1): The main hall was destroyed by a tsunami. ** The shrine also suffered tsunami damage during the Genroku, Kyōhō, and Kansei eras. * 1855 (Ansei 2): The main hall was rebuilt, and guardian lion-dog statues (komainu) were donated. * 1896 (Meiji 29): The worship hall (haiden) was constructed. * 1923 (Taishō 12): The worship hall was renovated. * 1971 (Shōwa 46): A shrine office and stone fence (tamagaki) were constructed. * 1982 (Shōwa 57): The main hall was designated a tangible cultural property by Urayasu City. * 2012 (Heisei 24): The first {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} was rebuilt.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/seiryuujinnjya/seibi-s-1.html Seiryū Shrine First Torii Rebuilding Ceremony, June 2, 2012] (Seiryū Shrine Official Website) == Grounds == * Main Hall - Designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Horie Level Reference Stone - Designated as a {{ill|Civil Engineering Heritage|ja|土木学会選奨土木遺産}}. It served as the zero level reference for the Edo River water gauge and is one of the oldest level reference stones in Japan, alongside the Iinuma Level Reference Stone in {{ill|Chōshi|ja|銚子市}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.ktr.mlit.go.jp/edogawa/edogawa00229.html|title=Horie Level Reference Stone|publisher=Edo River Office|accessdate=2022-12-19}} == Auxiliary Shrines == The grounds contain the following subordinate shrines: * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} * {{ill|Ryūjin Shrine|ja|龍神社}} * {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}} Shrine * {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} == festivals == [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 02.jpg|thumb|Summer purification ritual (chinowa kuguri)]] ; Annual Events * January 1: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * February 3: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分祭|Setsubun Festival=Setsubun Festival|lt=Setsubun Festival}} * February 11: Foundation Day Festival * February 17: {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} * June 15: festival ** Every four years, the grand festival (例大祭) is celebrated jointly by Seiryū Shrine, {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}, and {{ill|Toyouke Shrine|ja|豊受神社}}.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/urayasusannjya/ Urayasu Three Shrines] * June 30: {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|ja|夏越大祓|lt=Nagoshi-no-Ōharae}} * November 23: {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 31: Year-end Ōharae ; Monthly Festivals * 1st and 15th of each month: Monthly Festival (Tsukinami-sai) == Related Items == * The submarine [[:en:JS_Seiryū|JS Seiryū]] distributes amulets blessed at the shrine. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Seiryū-class submarine|ja|清瀧}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Seiryu jinja (urayasu)}} * [http://www.3ja.info/seiryujinja/ Urayasu Three Shrines - Seiryū Shrine] * [http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/kanko/716/ Chiba Prefecture Tourism - Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Urayasu City Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Urayasu]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kiyotaki Shrine (Urayasu City)]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kiyotaki Shrine (Urayasu City)]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:images]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[ja:%E6%B8%85%E7%80%A7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E6%B5%A6%E5%AE%89%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:清瀧神社]]

{{Shrine | name = Seiryū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Seiryu jinja urayasu haiden.JPG|250px|Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|39|43.8|N|139|53|28.2|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Urayasu|ja|浦安市}}, Horie 4-1-5 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|43.8|N|139|53|28.2|E|scale:5000}} | deity = {{ill|Ōwatatsumi|ja|大綿津見神}} | type = | consecration_year = {{ill|Kenkyū|ja|建久}} 7 (1196) | Main Hall = | Alternate name = | festival = June 15 | Rituals = }} [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 01.jpg|thumb|230px|The first torii, rebuilt in June 2012]] '''Seiryū Shrine''' (清瀧神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Urayasu|ja|浦安市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Horie district. == Overview == The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|祭神}} of the shrine is [[Watatsumi]], the god of the sea. The main hall, constructed in {{ill|Ansei|ja|安政}} 2 (1855), uses wood from large zelkova trees from the {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} and is designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. The architectural style is wooden, three-bay {{ill|nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, and the shrine crest is a right-facing mitsudomoe (triple swirl). == History == * {{ill|1196|ja|1196年}} (Kenkyū 7): The shrine was founded according to tradition. * {{ill|1299|ja|1299年}} (Einin 1): The main hall was destroyed by a tsunami. ** The shrine also suffered tsunami damage during the Genroku, Kyōhō, and Kansei eras. * 1855 (Ansei 2): The main hall was rebuilt, and guardian lion-dog statues (komainu) were donated. * 1896 (Meiji 29): The worship hall (haiden) was constructed. * 1923 (Taishō 12): The worship hall was renovated. * 1971 (Shōwa 46): A shrine office and stone fence (tamagaki) were constructed. * 1982 (Shōwa 57): The main hall was designated a tangible cultural property by Urayasu City. * 2012 (Heisei 24): The first {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} was rebuilt.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/seiryuujinnjya/seibi-s-1.html Seiryū Shrine First Torii Rebuilding Ceremony, June 2, 2012] (Seiryū Shrine Official Website) == Grounds == * Main Hall - Designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Horie Level Reference Stone - Designated as a {{ill|Civil Engineering Heritage|ja|土木学会選奨土木遺産}}. It served as the zero level reference for the Edo River water gauge and is one of the oldest level reference stones in Japan, alongside the Iinuma Level Reference Stone in {{ill|Chōshi|ja|銚子市}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.ktr.mlit.go.jp/edogawa/edogawa00229.html|title=Horie Level Reference Stone|publisher=Edo River Office|accessdate=2022-12-19}} == Auxiliary Shrines == The grounds contain the following subordinate shrines: * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} * {{ill|Ryūjin Shrine|ja|龍神社}} * {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}} Shrine * {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} == festivals == [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 02.jpg|thumb|Summer purification ritual (chinowa kuguri)]] ; Annual Events * January 1: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * February 3: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分祭|Setsubun Festival=Setsubun Festival|lt=Setsubun Festival}} * February 11: Foundation Day Festival * February 17: {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} * June 15: festival ** Every four years, the grand festival (例大祭) is celebrated jointly by Seiryū Shrine, {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}, and {{ill|Toyouke Shrine|ja|豊受神社}}.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/urayasusannjya/ Urayasu Three Shrines] * June 30: {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|ja|夏越大祓|lt=Nagoshi-no-Ōharae}} * November 23: {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 31: Year-end Ōharae ; Monthly Festivals * 1st and 15th of each month: Monthly Festival (Tsukinami-sai) == Related Items == * The submarine [[:en:JS_Seiryū|JS Seiryū]] distributes amulets blessed at the shrine. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Seiryū-class submarine|ja|清瀧}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Seiryu jinja (urayasu)}} * [http://www.3ja.info/seiryujinja/ Urayasu Three Shrines - Seiryū Shrine] * [http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/kanko/716/ Chiba Prefecture Tourism - Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Urayasu City Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Urayasu]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kiyotaki Shrine (Urayasu City)]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kiyotaki Shrine (Urayasu City)]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:images]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:Kiyotaki Shrine (Urayasu City)]] [[ja:%E6%B8%85%E7%80%A7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E6%B5%A6%E5%AE%89%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:清瀧神社]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Seiryū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Seiryu jinja urayasu haiden.JPG|250px|Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|39|43.8|N|139|53|28.2|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Urayasu|ja|浦安市}}, Horie 4-1-5 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|43.8|N|139|53|28.2|E|scale:5000}} | deity = {{ill|Ōwatatsumi|ja|大綿津見神}} | type = | consecration_year = {{ill|Kenkyū|ja|建久}} 7 (1196) | Main Hall = | Alternate name = | festival = June 15 | Rituals = }} [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 01.jpg|thumb|230px|The first torii, rebuilt in June 2012]] '''Seiryū Shrine''' (清瀧神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Urayasu|ja|浦安市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Horie district. == Overview == The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|祭神}} of the shrine is [[Watatsumi]], the god of the sea. The main hall, constructed in {{ill|Ansei|ja|安政}} 2 (1855), uses wood from large zelkova trees from the {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} and is designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. The architectural style is wooden, three-bay {{ill|nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, and the shrine crest is a right-facing mitsudomoe (triple swirl). == History == * {{ill|1196|ja|1196年}} (Kenkyū 7): The shrine was founded according to tradition. * {{ill|1299|ja|1299年}} (Einin 1): The main hall was destroyed by a tsunami. ** The shrine also suffered tsunami damage during the Genroku, Kyōhō, and Kansei eras. * 1855 (Ansei 2): The main hall was rebuilt, and guardian lion-dog statues (komainu) were donated. * 1896 (Meiji 29): The worship hall (haiden) was constructed. * 1923 (Taishō 12): The worship hall was renovated. * 1971 (Shōwa 46): A shrine office and stone fence (tamagaki) were constructed. * 1982 (Shōwa 57): The main hall was designated a tangible cultural property by Urayasu City. * 2012 (Heisei 24): The first {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} was rebuilt.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/seiryuujinnjya/seibi-s-1.html Seiryū Shrine First Torii Rebuilding Ceremony, June 2, 2012] (Seiryū Shrine Official Website) == Grounds == * Main Hall - Designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Horie Level Reference Stone - Designated as a {{ill|Civil Engineering Heritage|ja|土木学会選奨土木遺産}}. It served as the zero level reference for the Edo River water gauge and is one of the oldest level reference stones in Japan, alongside the Iinuma Level Reference Stone in {{ill|Chōshi|ja|銚子市}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.ktr.mlit.go.jp/edogawa/edogawa00229.html|title=Horie Level Reference Stone|publisher=Edo River Office|accessdate=2022-12-19}} == Auxiliary Shrines == The grounds contain the following subordinate shrines: * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} * {{ill|Ryūjin Shrine|ja|龍神社}} * {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}} Shrine * {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} == festivals == [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 02.jpg|thumb|Summer purification ritual (chinowa kuguri)]] ; Annual Events * January 1: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * February 3: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分祭|Setsubun Festival=Setsubun Festival|lt=Setsubun Festival}} * February 11: Foundation Day Festival * February 17: {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} * June 15: festival ** Every four years, the grand festival (例大祭) is celebrated jointly by Seiryū Shrine, {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}, and {{ill|Toyouke Shrine|ja|豊受神社}}.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/urayasusannjya/ Urayasu Three Shrines] * June 30: {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|ja|夏越大祓|lt=Nagoshi-no-Ōharae}} * November 23: {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 31: Year-end Ōharae ; Monthly Festivals * 1st and 15th of each month: Monthly Festival (Tsukinami-sai) == Related Items == * The submarine [[:en:JS_Seiryū|JS Seiryū]] distributes amulets blessed at the shrine. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Seiryū-class submarine|ja|清瀧}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Seiryu jinja (urayasu)}} * [http://www.3ja.info/seiryujinja/ Urayasu Three Shrines - Seiryū Shrine] * [http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/kanko/716/ Chiba Prefecture Tourism - Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Urayasu City Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Urayasu]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Kiyotaki Shrine (Urayasu City)-qq]] [[Category:Kiyotaki Shrine (Urayasu City)]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Kiyotaki Shrine (Urayasu City)-qq]] [[Category:Kiyotaki Shrine (Urayasu City)]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:images]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:Kiyotaki Shrine (Urayasu City)]] [[Category:Kiyotaki Shrine (Urayasu City)]][[ja:%E6%B8%85%E7%80%A7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E6%B5%A6%E5%AE%89%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:清瀧神社]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]

The architectural style is wooden, three-bay {{ill|nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, and the shrine crest is a right-facing mitsudomoe (triple swirl). == History == * {{ill|1196|ja|1196年}} (Kenkyū 7): The shrine was founded according to tradition. * {{ill|1299|ja|1299年}} (Einin 1): The main hall was destroyed by a tsunami. ** The shrine also suffered tsunami damage during the Genroku, Kyōhō, and Kansei eras. * 1855 (Ansei 2): The main hall was rebuilt, and guardian lion-dog statues (komainu) were donated. * 1896 (Meiji 29): The worship hall (haiden) was constructed. * 1923 (Taishō 12): The worship hall was renovated. * 1971 (Shōwa 46): A shrine office and stone fence (tamagaki) were constructed. * 1982 (Shōwa 57): The main hall was designated a tangible cultural property by Urayasu City. * 2012 (Heisei 24): The first {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} was rebuilt.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/seiryuujinnjya/seibi-s-1.html Seiryū Shrine First Torii Rebuilding Ceremony, June 2, 2012] (Seiryū Shrine Official Website) == Grounds == * Main Hall - Designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Horie Level Reference Stone - Designated as a {{ill|Civil Engineering Heritage|ja|土木学会選奨土木遺産}}. It served as the zero level reference for the Edo River water gauge and is one of the oldest level reference stones in Japan, alongside the Iinuma Level Reference Stone in {{ill|Chōshi|ja|銚子市}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.ktr.mlit.go.jp/edogawa/edogawa00229.html|title=Horie Level Reference Stone|publisher=Edo River Office|accessdate=2022-12-19}} == Auxiliary Shrines == The grounds contain the following subordinate shrines: * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} * {{ill|Ryūjin Shrine|ja|龍神社}} * {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}} Shrine * {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} == festivals == [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 02.jpg|thumb|Summer purification ritual (chinowa kuguri)]] ; Annual Events * January 1: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * February 3: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分祭|Setsubun Festival=Setsubun Festival|lt=Setsubun Festival}} * February 11: Foundation Day Festival * February 17: {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} * June 15: festival ** Every four years, the grand festival (例大祭) is celebrated jointly by Seiryū Shrine, {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}, and {{ill|Toyouke Shrine|ja|豊受神社}}.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/urayasusannjya/ Urayasu Three Shrines] * June 30: {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|ja|夏越大祓|lt=Nagoshi-no-Ōharae}} * November 23: {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 31: Year-end Ōharae ; The architectural style is wooden, three-bay {{ill|nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, and the shrine crest is a right-facing mitsudomoe (triple swirl). == History == * {{ill|1196|ja|1196年}} (Kenkyū 7): The shrine was founded according to tradition. * {{ill|1299|ja|1299年}} (Einin 1): The main hall was destroyed by a tsunami. ** The shrine also suffered tsunami damage during the Genroku, Kyōhō, and Kansei eras. * 1855 (Ansei 2): The main hall was rebuilt, and guardian lion-dog statues (komainu) were donated. * 1896 (Meiji 29): The worship hall (haiden) was constructed. * 1923 (Taishō 12): The worship hall was renovated. * 1971 (Shōwa 46): A shrine office and stone fence (tamagaki) were constructed. * 1982 (Shōwa 57): The main hall was designated a tangible cultural property by Urayasu City. * 2012 (Heisei 24): The first {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} was rebuilt.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/seiryuujinnjya/seibi-s-1.html Seiryū Shrine First Torii Rebuilding Ceremony, June 2, 2012] (Seiryū Shrine Official Website) == Grounds == * Main Hall - Designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Horie Level Reference Stone - Designated as a {{ill|Civil Engineering Heritage|ja|土木学会選奨土木遺産}}. It served as the zero level reference for the Edo River water gauge and is one of the oldest level reference stones in Japan, alongside the Iinuma Level Reference Stone in {{ill|Chōshi|ja|銚子市}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.ktr.mlit.go.jp/edogawa/edogawa00229.html|title=Horie Level Reference Stone|publisher=Edo River Office|accessdate=2022-12-19}} == Auxiliary Shrines == The grounds contain the following subordinate shrines: * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} * {{ill|Ryūjin Shrine|ja|龍神社}} * {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}} Shrine * {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} == festivals == [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 02.jpg|thumb|Summer purification ritual (chinowa kuguri)]] ; Annual Events * January 1: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * February 3: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分祭|Setsubun Festival=Setsubun Festival|lt=Setsubun Festival}} * February 11: Foundation Day Festival * February 17: {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} * June 15: festival ** Every four years, the grand festival (例大祭) is celebrated jointly by Seiryū Shrine, {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}, and {{ill|Toyouke Shrine|ja|豊受神社}}.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/urayasusannjya/ Urayasu Three Shrines] * June 30: {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|ja|夏越大祓|lt=Nagoshi-no-Ōharae}} * November 23: {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 31: Year-end Ōharae ; The main hall, constructed in {{ill|Ansei|ja|安政}} 2 (1855), uses wood from large zelkova trees from the {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} and is designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. The architectural style is wooden, three-bay {{ill|nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, and the shrine crest is a right-facing mitsudomoe (triple swirl). == History == * {{ill|1196|ja|1196年}} (Kenkyū 7): The shrine was founded according to tradition. * {{ill|1299|ja|1299年}} (Einin 1): The main hall was destroyed by a tsunami. ** The shrine also suffered tsunami damage during the Genroku, Kyōhō, and Kansei eras. * 1855 (Ansei 2): The main hall was rebuilt, and guardian lion-dog statues (komainu) were donated. * 1896 (Meiji 29): The worship hall (haiden) was constructed. * 1923 (Taishō 12): The worship hall was renovated. * 1971 (Shōwa 46): A shrine office and stone fence (tamagaki) were constructed. * 1982 (Shōwa 57): The main hall was designated a tangible cultural property by Urayasu City. * 2012 (Heisei 24): The first {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} was rebuilt.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/seiryuujinnjya/seibi-s-1.html Seiryū Shrine First Torii Rebuilding Ceremony, June 2, 2012] (Seiryū Shrine Official Website) == Grounds == * Main Hall - Designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Horie Level Reference Stone - Designated as a {{ill|Civil Engineering Heritage|ja|土木学会選奨土木遺産}}. {{Shrine | name = Seiryū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Seiryu jinja urayasu haiden.JPG|250px|Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|39|43.8|N|139|53|28.2|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Urayasu|ja|浦安市}}, Horie 4-1-5 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|43.8|N|139|53|28.2|E|scale:5000}} | deity = {{ill|Ōwatatsumi|ja|大綿津見神}} | type = | consecration_year = {{ill|Kenkyū|ja|建久}} 7 (1196) | Main Hall = | Alternate name = | festival = June 15 | Rituals = }} [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 01.jpg|thumb|230px|The first torii, rebuilt in June 2012]] '''Seiryū Shrine''' (清瀧神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Urayasu|ja|浦安市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Horie district. == Overview == The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|祭神}} of the shrine is [[Watatsumi]], the god of the sea. The main hall, constructed in {{ill|Ansei|ja|安政}} 2 (1855), uses wood from large zelkova trees from the {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} and is designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. The architectural style is wooden, three-bay {{ill|nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, and the shrine crest is a right-facing mitsudomoe (triple swirl). == History == * {{ill|1196|ja|1196年}} (Kenkyū 7): The shrine was founded according to tradition. * {{ill|1299|ja|1299年}} (Einin 1): The main hall was destroyed by a tsunami. ** The shrine also suffered tsunami damage during the Genroku, Kyōhō, and Kansei eras. * 1855 (Ansei 2): The main hall was rebuilt, and guardian lion-dog statues (komainu) were donated. * 1896 (Meiji 29): The worship hall (haiden) was constructed. * 1923 (Taishō 12): The worship hall was renovated. * 1971 (Shōwa 46): A shrine office and stone fence (tamagaki) were constructed. * 1982 (Shōwa 57): The main hall was designated a tangible cultural property by Urayasu City. * 2012 (Heisei 24): The first {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} was rebuilt.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/seiryuujinnjya/seibi-s-1.html Seiryū Shrine First Torii Rebuilding Ceremony, June 2, 2012] (Seiryū Shrine Official Website) == Grounds == * Main Hall - Designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Horie Level Reference Stone - Designated as a {{ill|Civil Engineering Heritage|ja|土木学会選奨土木遺産}}. It served as the zero level reference for the Edo River water gauge and is one of the oldest level reference stones in Japan, alongside the Iinuma Level Reference Stone in {{ill|Chōshi|ja|銚子市}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.ktr.mlit.go.jp/edogawa/edogawa00229.html|title=Horie Level Reference Stone|publisher=Edo River Office|accessdate=2022-12-19}} == Auxiliary Shrines == The grounds contain the following subordinate shrines: * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} * {{ill|Ryūjin Shrine|ja|龍神社}} * {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}} Shrine * {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} == festivals == [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 02.jpg|thumb|Summer purification ritual (chinowa kuguri)]] ; Annual Events * January 1: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * February 3: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分祭|Setsubun Festival=Setsubun Festival|lt=Setsubun Festival}} * February 11: Foundation Day Festival * February 17: {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} * June 15: festival ** Every four years, the grand festival (例大祭) is celebrated jointly by Seiryū Shrine, {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}, and {{ill|Toyouke Shrine|ja|豊受神社}}.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/urayasusannjya/ Urayasu Three Shrines] * June 30: {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|ja|夏越大祓|lt=Nagoshi-no-Ōharae}} * November 23: {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 31: Year-end Ōharae ; Monthly Festivals * 1st and 15th of each month: Monthly Festival (Tsukinami-sai) == Related Items == * The submarine [[:en:JS_Seiryū|JS Seiryū]] distributes amulets blessed at the shrine. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Seiryū-class submarine|ja|清瀧}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Seiryu jinja (urayasu)}} * [http://www.3ja.info/seiryujinja/ Urayasu Three Shrines - Seiryū Shrine] * [http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/kanko/716/ Chiba Prefecture Tourism - Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Urayasu City Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in {{Shrine | name = Seiryū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Seiryu jinja urayasu haiden.JPG|250px|Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|39|43.8|N|139|53|28.2|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Urayasu|ja|浦安市}}, Horie 4-1-5 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|43.8|N|139|53|28.2|E|scale:5000}} | deity = {{ill|Ōwatatsumi|ja|大綿津見神}} | type = | consecration_year = {{ill|Kenkyū|ja|建久}} 7 (1196) | Main Hall = | Alternate name = | festival = June 15 | Rituals = }} [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 01.jpg|thumb|230px|The first torii, rebuilt in June 2012]] '''Seiryū Shrine''' (清瀧神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Urayasu|ja|浦安市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Horie district. == Overview == The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|祭神}} of the shrine is [[Watatsumi]], the god of the sea. The main hall, constructed in {{ill|Ansei|ja|安政}} 2 (1855), uses wood from large zelkova trees from the {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} and is designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. The architectural style is wooden, three-bay {{ill|nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, and the shrine crest is a right-facing mitsudomoe (triple swirl). == History == * {{ill|1196|ja|1196年}} (Kenkyū 7): The shrine was founded according to tradition. * {{ill|1299|ja|1299年}} (Einin 1): The main hall was destroyed by a tsunami. ** The shrine also suffered tsunami damage during the Genroku, Kyōhō, and Kansei eras. * 1855 (Ansei 2): The main hall was rebuilt, and guardian lion-dog statues (komainu) were donated. * 1896 (Meiji 29): The worship hall (haiden) was constructed. * 1923 (Taishō 12): The worship hall was renovated. * 1971 (Shōwa 46): A shrine office and stone fence (tamagaki) were constructed. * 1982 (Shōwa 57): The main hall was designated a tangible cultural property by Urayasu City. * 2012 (Heisei 24): The first {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} was rebuilt.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/seiryuujinnjya/seibi-s-1.html Seiryū Shrine First Torii Rebuilding Ceremony, June 2, 2012] (Seiryū Shrine Official Website) == Grounds == * Main Hall - Designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Horie Level Reference Stone - Designated as a {{ill|Civil Engineering Heritage|ja|土木学会選奨土木遺産}}. It served as the zero level reference for the Edo River water gauge and is one of the oldest level reference stones in Japan, alongside the Iinuma Level Reference Stone in {{ill|Chōshi|ja|銚子市}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.ktr.mlit.go.jp/edogawa/edogawa00229.html|title=Horie Level Reference Stone|publisher=Edo River Office|accessdate=2022-12-19}} == Auxiliary Shrines == The grounds contain the following subordinate shrines: * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} * {{ill|Ryūjin Shrine|ja|龍神社}} * {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}} Shrine * {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} == festivals == [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 02.jpg|thumb|Summer purification ritual (chinowa kuguri)]] ; Annual Events * January 1: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * February 3: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分祭|Setsubun Festival=Setsubun Festival|lt=Setsubun Festival}} * February 11: Foundation Day Festival * February 17: {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} * June 15: festival ** Every four years, the grand festival (例大祭) is celebrated jointly by Seiryū Shrine, {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}, and {{ill|Toyouke Shrine|ja|豊受神社}}.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/urayasusannjya/ Urayasu Three Shrines] * June 30: {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|ja|夏越大祓|lt=Nagoshi-no-Ōharae}} * November 23: {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 31: Year-end Ōharae ; Monthly Festivals * 1st and 15th of each month: Monthly Festival (Tsukinami-sai) == Related Items == * The submarine [[:en:JS_Seiryū|JS Seiryū]] distributes amulets blessed at the shrine. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Seiryū-class submarine|ja|清瀧}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Seiryu jinja (urayasu)}} * [http://www.3ja.info/seiryujinja/ Urayasu Three Shrines - Seiryū Shrine] * [http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/kanko/716/ Chiba Prefecture Tourism - Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Urayasu City Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in The main hall, constructed in {{ill|Ansei|ja|安政}} 2 (1855), uses wood from large zelkova trees from the {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} and is designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. The architectural style is wooden, three-bay {{ill|nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, and the shrine crest is a right-facing mitsudomoe (triple swirl). == History == * {{ill|1196|ja|1196年}} (Kenkyū 7): The shrine was founded according to tradition. * {{ill|1299|ja|1299年}} (Einin 1): The main hall was destroyed by a tsunami. ** The shrine also suffered tsunami damage during the Genroku, Kyōhō, and Kansei eras. * 1855 (Ansei 2): The main hall was rebuilt, and guardian lion-dog statues (komainu) were donated. * 1896 (Meiji 29): The worship hall (haiden) was constructed. * 1923 (Taishō 12): The worship hall was renovated. * 1971 (Shōwa 46): A shrine office and stone fence (tamagaki) were constructed. * 1982 (Shōwa 57): The main hall was designated a tangible cultural property by Urayasu City. * 2012 (Heisei 24): The first {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} was rebuilt.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/seiryuujinnjya/seibi-s-1.html Seiryū Shrine First Torii Rebuilding Ceremony, June 2, 2012] (Seiryū Shrine Official Website) == Grounds == * Main Hall - Designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Horie Level Reference Stone - Designated as a {{ill|Civil Engineering Heritage|ja|土木学会選奨土木遺産}}. It served as the zero level reference for the Edo River water gauge and is one of the oldest level reference stones in Japan, alongside the Iinuma Level Reference Stone in {{ill|Chōshi|ja|銚子市}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.ktr.mlit.go.jp/edogawa/edogawa00229.html|title=Horie Level Reference Stone|publisher=Edo River Office|accessdate=2022-12-19}} == Auxiliary Shrines == The grounds contain the Festival|lt=Setsubun Festival}} * February 11: Foundation Day Festival * February 17: {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} * June 15: festival ** Every four years, the grand festival (例大祭) is celebrated jointly by revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Seiryū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Seiryu jinja urayasu haiden.JPG|250px|Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|39|43.8|N|139|53|28.2|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Urayasu|ja|浦安市}}, Horie 4-1-5 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|43.8|N|139|53|28.2|E|scale:5000}} | deity = {{ill|Ōwatatsumi|ja|大綿津見神}} | type = | consecration_year = {{ill|Kenkyū|ja|建久}} 7 (1196) | Main Hall = | Alternate name = | festival = June 15 | Rituals = }} [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 01.jpg|thumb|230px|The first torii, rebuilt in June 2012]] '''Seiryū Shrine''' (清瀧神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Urayasu|ja|浦安市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Horie district. == Overview == The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|祭神}} of the shrine is [[Watatsumi]], the god of the sea. The main hall, constructed in {{ill|Ansei|ja|安政}} 2 (1855), uses wood from large zelkova trees from the {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} and is designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. The architectural style is wooden, three-bay {{ill|nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, and the shrine crest is a right-facing mitsudomoe (triple swirl). == History == * {{ill|1196|ja|1196年}} (Kenkyū 7): The shrine was founded according to tradition. * {{ill|1299|ja|1299年}} (Einin 1): The main hall was destroyed by a tsunami. ** The shrine also suffered tsunami damage during the Genroku, Kyōhō, and Kansei eras. * 1855 (Ansei 2): The main hall was rebuilt, and guardian lion-dog statues (komainu) were donated. * 1896 (Meiji 29): The worship hall (haiden) was constructed. * 1923 (Taishō 12): The worship hall was renovated. * 1971 (Shōwa 46): A shrine office and stone fence (tamagaki) were constructed. * 1982 (Shōwa 57): The main hall was designated a tangible cultural property by Urayasu City. * 2012 (Heisei 24): The first {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} was rebuilt.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/seiryuujinnjya/seibi-s-1.html Seiryū Shrine First Torii Rebuilding Ceremony, June 2, 2012] (Seiryū Shrine Official Website) == Grounds == * Main Hall - Designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Horie Level Reference Stone - Designated as a {{ill|Civil Engineering Heritage|ja|土木学会選奨土木遺産}}. It served as the zero level reference for the Edo River water gauge and is one of the oldest level reference stones in Japan, alongside the Iinuma Level Reference Stone in {{ill|Chōshi|ja|銚子市}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.ktr.mlit.go.jp/edogawa/edogawa00229.html|title=Horie Level Reference Stone|publisher=Edo River Office|accessdate=2022-12-19}} == Auxiliary Shrines == The grounds contain the following subordinate shrines: * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} * {{ill|Ryūjin Shrine|ja|龍神社}} * {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}} Shrine * {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} == festivals == [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 02.jpg|thumb|Summer purification ritual (chinowa kuguri)]] ; Annual Events * January 1: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * February 3: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分祭|Setsubun Festival=Setsubun Festival|lt=Setsubun Festival}} * February 11: Foundation Day Festival * February 17: {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} * June 15: festival ** Every four years, the grand festival (例大祭) is celebrated jointly by Seiryū Shrine, {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}, and {{ill|Toyouke Shrine|ja|豊受神社}}.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/urayasusannjya/ Urayasu Three Shrines] * June 30: {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|ja|夏越大祓|lt=Nagoshi-no-Ōharae}} * November 23: {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 31: Year-end Ōharae ; Monthly Festivals * 1st and 15th of each month: Monthly Festival (Tsukinami-sai) == Related Items == * The submarine [[:en:JS_Seiryū|JS Seiryū]] distributes amulets blessed at the shrine. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Seiryū-class submarine|ja|清瀧}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Seiryu jinja (urayasu)}} * [http://www.3ja.info/seiryujinja/ Urayasu Three Shrines - Seiryū Shrine] * [http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/kanko/716/ Chiba Prefecture Tourism - Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Urayasu City Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in {{Shrine | name = Seiryū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Seiryu jinja urayasu haiden.JPG|250px|Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|39|43.8|N|139|53|28.2|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Urayasu|ja|浦安市}}, Horie 4-1-5 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|43.8|N|139|53|28.2|E|scale:5000}} | deity = {{ill|Ōwatatsumi|ja|大綿津見神}} | type = | consecration_year = {{ill|Kenkyū|ja|建久}} 7 (1196) | Main Hall = | Alternate name = | festival = June 15 | Rituals = }} [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 01.jpg|thumb|230px|The first torii, rebuilt in June 2012]] '''Seiryū Shrine''' (清瀧神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Urayasu|ja|浦安市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Horie district. == Overview == The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|祭神}} of the shrine is [[Watatsumi]], the god of the sea. The main hall, constructed in {{ill|Ansei|ja|安政}} 2 (1855), uses wood from large zelkova trees from the {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} and is designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. The architectural style is wooden, three-bay {{ill|nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, and the shrine crest is a right-facing mitsudomoe (triple swirl). == History == * {{ill|1196|ja|1196年}} (Kenkyū 7): The shrine was founded according to tradition. * {{ill|1299|ja|1299年}} (Einin 1): The main hall was destroyed by a tsunami. ** The shrine also suffered tsunami damage during the Genroku, Kyōhō, and Kansei eras. * 1855 (Ansei 2): The main hall was rebuilt, and guardian lion-dog statues (komainu) were donated. * 1896 (Meiji 29): The worship hall (haiden) was constructed. * 1923 (Taishō 12): The worship hall was renovated. * 1971 (Shōwa 46): A shrine office and stone fence (tamagaki) were constructed. * 1982 (Shōwa 57): The main hall was designated a tangible cultural property by Urayasu City. * 2012 (Heisei 24): The first {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} was rebuilt.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/seiryuujinnjya/seibi-s-1.html Seiryū Shrine First Torii Rebuilding Ceremony, June 2, 2012] (Seiryū Shrine Official Website) == Grounds == * Main Hall - Designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Horie Level Reference Stone - Designated as a {{ill|Civil Engineering Heritage|ja|土木学会選奨土木遺産}}. It served as the zero level reference for the Edo River water gauge and is one of the oldest level reference stones in Japan, alongside the Iinuma Level Reference Stone in {{ill|Chōshi|ja|銚子市}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.ktr.mlit.go.jp/edogawa/edogawa00229.html|title=Horie Level Reference Stone|publisher=Edo River Office|accessdate=2022-12-19}} == Auxiliary Shrines == The grounds contain the following subordinate shrines: * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} * {{ill|Ryūjin Shrine|ja|龍神社}} * {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}} Shrine * {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} == festivals == [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 02.jpg|thumb|Summer purification ritual (chinowa kuguri)]] ; Annual Events * January 1: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * February 3: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分祭|Setsubun Festival=Setsubun Festival|lt=Setsubun Festival}} * February 11: Foundation Day Festival * February 17: {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} * June 15: festival ** Every four years, the grand festival (例大祭) is celebrated jointly by Seiryū Shrine, {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}, and {{ill|Toyouke Shrine|ja|豊受神社}}.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/urayasusannjya/ Urayasu Three Shrines] * June 30: {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|ja|夏越大祓|lt=Nagoshi-no-Ōharae}} * November 23: {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 31: Year-end Ōharae ; Monthly Festivals * 1st and 15th of each month: Monthly Festival (Tsukinami-sai) == Related Items == * The submarine [[:en:JS_Seiryū|JS Seiryū]] distributes amulets blessed at the shrine. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Seiryū-class submarine|ja|清瀧}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Seiryu jinja (urayasu)}} * [http://www.3ja.info/seiryujinja/ Urayasu Three Shrines - Seiryū Shrine] * [http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/kanko/716/ Chiba Prefecture Tourism - Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Urayasu City Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|39|43.8|N|139|53|28.2|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Urayasu|ja|浦安市}}, Horie 4-1-5 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|43.8|N|139|53|28.2|E|scale:5000}} | deity = {{ill|Ōwatatsumi|ja|大綿津見神}} | type = | consecration_year = {{ill|Kenkyū|ja|建久}} 7 (1196) | Main Hall = | Alternate name = | festival = June 15 | Rituals = }} [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 01.jpg|thumb|230px|The first torii, rebuilt in June 2012]] '''Seiryū Shrine''' (清瀧神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Urayasu|ja|浦安市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Horie district. == Overview == The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|祭神}} of the shrine is [[Watatsumi]], the god of the sea. The main hall, constructed in {{ill|Ansei|ja|安政}} 2 (1855), uses wood from large zelkova trees from the {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} and is designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. The architectural style is wooden, three-bay {{ill|nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, and the shrine crest is a right-facing mitsudomoe (triple swirl). == History == * {{ill|1196|ja|1196年}} (Kenkyū 7): The shrine was founded according to tradition. * {{ill|1299|ja|1299年}} (Einin 1): The main hall was destroyed by a tsunami. ** The shrine also suffered tsunami damage during the Genroku, Kyōhō, and Kansei eras. * 1855 (Ansei 2): The main hall was rebuilt, and guardian lion-dog statues (komainu) were donated. * 1896 (Meiji 29): The worship hall (haiden) was constructed. * 1923 (Taishō 12): The worship hall was renovated. * 1971 (Shōwa 46): A shrine office and stone fence (tamagaki) were constructed. * 1982 (Shōwa 57): The main hall was designated a tangible cultural property by Urayasu City. * 2012 (Heisei 24): The first {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} was rebuilt.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/seiryuujinnjya/seibi-s-1.html Seiryū Shrine First Torii Rebuilding Ceremony, June 2, 2012] (Seiryū Shrine Official Website) == Grounds == * Main Hall - Designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Horie Level Reference Stone - Designated as a {{ill|Civil Engineering Heritage|ja|土木学会選奨土木遺産}}. It served as the zero level reference for the Edo River water gauge and is one of the oldest level reference stones in Japan, alongside the Iinuma Level Reference Stone in {{ill|Chōshi|ja|銚子市}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.ktr.mlit.go.jp/edogawa/edogawa00229.html|title=Horie Level Reference Stone|publisher=Edo River Office|accessdate=2022-12-19}} == Auxiliary Shrines == The grounds contain the following subordinate shrines: * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} * {{ill|Ryūjin Shrine|ja|龍神社}} * {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}} Shrine * {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} == festivals == [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 02.jpg|thumb|Summer purification ritual (chinowa kuguri)]] ; Annual Events * January 1: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * February 3: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分祭|Setsubun Festival=Setsubun Festival|lt=Setsubun Festival}} * February 11: Foundation Day Festival * February 17: {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} * June 15: festival ** Every four years, the grand festival (例大祭) is celebrated jointly by Seiryū Shrine, {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}, and {{ill|Toyouke Shrine|ja|豊受神社}}.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/urayasusannjya/ Urayasu Three Shrines] * June 30: {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|ja|夏越大祓|lt=Nagoshi-no-Ōharae}} * November 23: {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 31: Year-end Ōharae ; Monthly Festivals * 1st and 15th of each month: Monthly Festival (Tsukinami-sai) == Related Items == * The submarine [[:en:JS_Seiryū|JS Seiryū]] distributes amulets blessed at the shrine. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Seiryū-class submarine|ja|清瀧}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Seiryu jinja (urayasu)}} * [http://www.3ja.info/seiryujinja/ Urayasu Three Shrines - Seiryū Shrine] * [http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/kanko/716/ following subordinate shrines: * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} * {{ill|Ryūjin Shrine|ja|龍神社}} * {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}} Shrine * {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} == festivals == [[File:Seiryu jinja Festival|lt=Setsubun Festival}} * February 11: Foundation Day Festival * February 17: {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} * June 15: festival ** Every four years, the grand festival (例大祭) is celebrated jointly by Seiryū Shrine, {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}, and {{ill|Toyouke Shrine|ja|豊受神社}}.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/urayasusannjya/ Urayasu Three Shrines] * June 30: {{Shrine | name = Seiryū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Seiryu jinja urayasu haiden.JPG|250px|Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|39|43.8|N|139|53|28.2|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Urayasu|ja|浦安市}}, Horie 4-1-5 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|43.8|N|139|53|28.2|E|scale:5000}} | deity = {{ill|Ōwatatsumi|ja|大綿津見神}} | type = | consecration_year = {{ill|Kenkyū|ja|建久}} 7 (1196) | Main Hall = | Alternate name = | festival = June 15 | Rituals = }} [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 01.jpg|thumb|230px|The first torii, rebuilt in June 2012]] '''Seiryū Shrine''' (清瀧神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Urayasu|ja|浦安市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Horie district. == Overview == The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|祭神}} of the shrine is [[Watatsumi]], the god of the sea. The main hall, constructed in {{ill|Ansei|ja|安政}} 2 (1855), uses wood from large zelkova trees from the {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} and is designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. The architectural style is wooden, three-bay {{ill|nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, and the shrine crest is a right-facing mitsudomoe (triple swirl). == History == * {{ill|1196|ja|1196年}} (Kenkyū 7): The shrine was founded according to tradition. * {{ill|1299|ja|1299年}} (Einin 1): The main hall was destroyed by a tsunami. ** The shrine also suffered tsunami damage during the Genroku, Kyōhō, and Kansei eras. * 1855 (Ansei 2): The main hall was rebuilt, and guardian lion-dog statues (komainu) were donated. * 1896 (Meiji 29): The worship hall (haiden) was constructed. * 1923 (Taishō 12): The worship hall was renovated. * 1971 (Shōwa 46): A shrine office and stone fence (tamagaki) were constructed. * 1982 (Shōwa 57): The main hall was designated a tangible cultural property by Urayasu City. * 2012 (Heisei 24): The first {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} was rebuilt.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/seiryuujinnjya/seibi-s-1.html Seiryū Shrine First Torii Rebuilding Ceremony, June 2, 2012] (Seiryū Shrine Official Website) == Grounds == * Main Hall - Designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Horie Level Reference Stone - Designated as a {{ill|Civil Engineering Heritage|ja|土木学会選奨土木遺産}}. It served as the zero level reference for the Edo River water gauge and is one of the oldest level reference stones in Japan, alongside the Iinuma Level Reference Stone in {{ill|Chōshi|ja|銚子市}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.ktr.mlit.go.jp/edogawa/edogawa00229.html|title=Horie Level Reference Stone|publisher=Edo River Office|accessdate=2022-12-19}} == Auxiliary Shrines == The grounds contain the following subordinate shrines: * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} * {{ill|Ryūjin Shrine|ja|龍神社}} * {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}} Shrine * {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} == festivals == [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 02.jpg|thumb|Summer purification ritual (chinowa kuguri)]] ; Annual Events * January 1: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * February 3: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分祭|Setsubun Festival=Setsubun Festival|lt=Setsubun Festival}} * February 11: Foundation Day Festival * February 17: {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} * June 15: festival ** Every four years, the grand festival (例大祭) is celebrated jointly by Seiryū Shrine, {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}, and {{ill|Toyouke Shrine|ja|豊受神社}}.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/urayasusannjya/ Urayasu Three Shrines] * June 30: {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|ja|夏越大祓|lt=Nagoshi-no-Ōharae}} * November 23: {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 31: Year-end Ōharae ; Monthly Festivals * 1st and 15th of each month: Monthly Festival (Tsukinami-sai) == Related Items == * The submarine [[:en:JS_Seiryū|JS Seiryū]] distributes amulets blessed at the shrine. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Seiryū-class
