Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|39|43.8|N|139|53|28.2|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], [[:en:Urayasu|Urayasu]], Horie 4-1-5 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|43.8|N|139|53|28.2|E|scale:5000}} | deity = {{ill|Ōwatatsumi|ja|大綿津見神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwatatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ|1=Watatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1474187}} | type = | consecration_year = [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 7 (1196) | Main Hall = | Alternate name = | festival = June 15 | Rituals = }} [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 01.jpg|thumb|230px|The first torii, rebuilt in June 2012]] '''Seiryū Shrine''' (清瀧神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Urayasu|Urayasu]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], in the Horie district. == Overview == The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|ja|祭神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|qq=}} of the shrine is [[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]], the god of the sea. The main hall, constructed in [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 2 (1855), uses wood from large zelkova trees from the [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]] and is designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. The architectural style is wooden, three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|nagare-zukuri]], and the shrine crest is a right-facing mitsudomoe (triple swirl). == History == * [[:en:1196|1196]] (Kenkyū 7): The shrine was founded according to tradition. * [[:en:1299|1299]] (Einin 1): The main hall was destroyed by a tsunami. ** The shrine also suffered tsunami damage during the Genroku, Kyōhō, and Kansei eras. * 1855 (Ansei 2): The main hall was rebuilt, and guardian lion-dog statues (komainu) were donated. * 1896 (Meiji 29): The worship hall (haiden) was constructed. * 1923 (Taishō 12): The worship hall was renovated. * 1971 (Shōwa 46): A shrine office and stone fence (tamagaki) were constructed. * 1982 (Shōwa 57): The main hall was designated a tangible cultural property by Urayasu City. * 2012 (Heisei 24): The first [[:en:Torii|torii]] was rebuilt.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/seiryuujinnjya/seibi-s-1.html Seiryū Shrine First Torii Rebuilding Ceremony, June 2, 2012] (Seiryū Shrine Official Website) == Grounds == * Main Hall - Designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Horie Level Reference Stone - Designated as a {{ill|Civil Engineering Heritage|ja|土木学会選奨土木遺産|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Engineering Heritage|qq=|1=JSCE Civil Engineering Heritage Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423672}}. It served as the zero level reference for the Edo River water gauge and is one of the oldest level reference stones in Japan, alongside the Iinuma Level Reference Stone in [[:en:Chōshi|Chōshi]].{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.ktr.mlit.go.jp/edogawa/edogawa00229.html|title=Horie Level Reference Stone|publisher=Edo River Office|accessdate=2022-12-19}} == Auxiliary Shrines == The grounds contain the following subordinate shrines: * [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] * {{ill|Ryūjin Shrine|ja|龍神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūjin Shrine|qq=|1=Ryū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11679331}} * [[:en:Dōsojin|Dōsojin]] Shrine * [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]] == festivals == [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 02.jpg|thumb|Summer purification ritual (chinowa kuguri)]] ; Annual Events * January 1: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} * February 3: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分祭|Setsubun Festival=Setsubun Festival|lt=Setsubun Festival|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分}} * February 11: Foundation Day Festival * February 17: [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] * June 15: festival ** Every four years, the grand festival (例大祭) is celebrated jointly by Seiryū Shrine, [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]], and {{ill|Toyouke Shrine|ja|豊受神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Shrine|qq=}}.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/urayasusannjya/ Urayasu Three Shrines] * June 30: {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|ja|夏越大祓|lt=Nagoshi-no-Ōharae|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae-shiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓}} * November 23: [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] * December 31: Year-end Ōharae ; Monthly Festivals * 1st and 15th of each month: Monthly Festival (Tsukinami-sai) == Related Items == * The submarine [[:en:JS_Seiryū|JS Seiryū]] distributes amulets blessed at the shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Seiryū-class submarine|ja|清瀧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū-class submarine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 清滝|1=Seiryū Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561300}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Seiryu jinja (urayasu)}} * [http://www.3ja.info/seiryujinja/ Urayasu Three Shrines - Seiryū Shrine] * [http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/kanko/716/ Chiba Prefecture Tourism - Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|清瀧神社 (浦安市)|version=104121126|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kiyotaki Shrine (Urayasu City)]] [[sn:E6%B8%85%E7%80%A7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E6%B5%A6%E5%AE%89%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:清瀧神社]] [[Category:Municipal History of Urayasu]] {{Commons category|Seiryu-jinja (Urayasu)}} [[ja:清瀧神社 (浦安市)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11561325]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:19, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Seiryū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Seiryu jinja urayasu haiden.JPG|250px|Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|39|43.8|N|139|53|28.2|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], [[:en:Urayasu|Urayasu]], Horie 4-1-5 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|43.8|N|139|53|28.2|E|scale:5000}} | deity = {{ill|Ōwatatsumi|ja|大綿津見神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwatatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ|1=Watatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1474187}} | type = | consecration_year = [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 7 (1196) | Main Hall = | Alternate name = | festival = June 15 | Rituals = }} [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 01.jpg|thumb|230px|The first torii, rebuilt in June 2012]] '''Seiryū Shrine''' (清瀧神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Urayasu|Urayasu]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], in the Horie district. == Overview == The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|ja|祭神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|qq=}} of the shrine is [[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]], the god of the sea. The main hall, constructed in [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 2 (1855), uses wood from large zelkova trees from the [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]] and is designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. The architectural style is wooden, three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|nagare-zukuri]], and the shrine crest is a right-facing mitsudomoe (triple swirl). == History == * [[:en:1196|1196]] (Kenkyū 7): The shrine was founded according to tradition. * [[:en:1299|1299]] (Einin 1): The main hall was destroyed by a tsunami. ** The shrine also suffered tsunami damage during the Genroku, Kyōhō, and Kansei eras. * 1855 (Ansei 2): The main hall was rebuilt, and guardian lion-dog statues (komainu) were donated. * 1896 (Meiji 29): The worship hall (haiden) was constructed. * 1923 (Taishō 12): The worship hall was renovated. * 1971 (Shōwa 46): A shrine office and stone fence (tamagaki) were constructed. * 1982 (Shōwa 57): The main hall was designated a tangible cultural property by Urayasu City. * 2012 (Heisei 24): The first [[:en:Torii|torii]] was rebuilt.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/seiryuujinnjya/seibi-s-1.html Seiryū Shrine First Torii Rebuilding Ceremony, June 2, 2012] (Seiryū Shrine Official Website) == Grounds == * Main Hall - Designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Horie Level Reference Stone - Designated as a {{ill|Civil Engineering Heritage|ja|土木学会選奨土木遺産|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Engineering Heritage|qq=|1=JSCE Civil Engineering Heritage Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423672}}. It served as the zero level reference for the Edo River water gauge and is one of the oldest level reference stones in Japan, alongside the Iinuma Level Reference Stone in [[:en:Chōshi|Chōshi]].{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.ktr.mlit.go.jp/edogawa/edogawa00229.html|title=Horie Level Reference Stone|publisher=Edo River Office|accessdate=2022-12-19}} == Auxiliary Shrines == The grounds contain the following subordinate shrines: * [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] * {{ill|Ryūjin Shrine|ja|龍神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūjin Shrine|qq=|1=Ryūjin Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11679331}} * [[:en:Dōsojin|Dōsojin]] Shrine * [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]] == festivals == [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 02.jpg|thumb|Summer purification ritual (chinowa kuguri)]] ; Annual Events * January 1: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} * February 3: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分祭|Setsubun Festival=Setsubun Festival|lt=Setsubun Festival|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分}} * February 11: Foundation Day Festival * February 17: [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] * June 15: festival ** Every four years, the grand festival (例大祭) is celebrated jointly by Seiryū Shrine, [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]], and {{ill|Toyouke Shrine|ja|豊受神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Shrine|qq=}}.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/urayasusannjya/ Urayasu Three Shrines] * June 30: {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|ja|夏越大祓|lt=Nagoshi-no-Ōharae|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae-shiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓}} * November 23: [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] * December 31: Year-end Ōharae ; Monthly Festivals * 1st and 15th of each month: Monthly Festival (Tsukinami-sai) == Related Items == * The submarine [[:en:JS_Seiryū|JS Seiryū]] distributes amulets blessed at the shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Seiryū-class submarine|ja|清瀧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū-class submarine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 清滝|1=Seiryū Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561300}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Seiryu jinja (urayasu)}} * [http://www.3ja.info/seiryujinja/ Urayasu Three Shrines - Seiryū Shrine] * [http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/kanko/716/ Chiba Prefecture Tourism - Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|清瀧神社 (浦安市)|version=104121126|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:清瀧神社]] [[Category:Municipal History of Urayasu]] {{Commons category|Seiryu-jinja (Urayasu)}} [[ja:清瀧神社 (浦安市)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q11561325}}

The main hall, constructed in [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 2 (1855), uses wood from large zelkova trees from the [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]] and is designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. The architectural style is wooden, three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|nagare-zukuri]], and the shrine crest is a right-facing mitsudomoe (triple swirl). == History == * [[:en:1196|1196]] (Kenkyū 7): The shrine was founded according to tradition. * [[:en:1299|1299]] (Einin 1): The main hall was destroyed by a tsunami. ** The shrine also suffered tsunami damage during the Genroku, Kyōhō, and Kansei eras. * 1855 (Ansei 2): The main hall was rebuilt, and guardian lion-dog statues (komainu) were donated. * 1896 (Meiji 29): The worship hall (haiden) was constructed. * 1923 (Taishō 12): The worship hall was renovated. * 1971 (Shōwa 46): A shrine office and stone fence (tamagaki) were constructed. * 1982 (Shōwa 57): The main hall was designated a tangible cultural property by Urayasu City. * 2012 (Heisei 24): The first [[:en:Torii|torii]] was rebuilt.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/seiryuujinnjya/seibi-s-1.html Seiryū Shrine First Torii Rebuilding Ceremony, June 2, 2012] (Seiryū Shrine Official Website) == Grounds == * Main Hall - Designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Horie Level Reference Stone - Designated as a {{ill|Civil Engineering Heritage|ja|土木学会選奨土木遺産|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Engineering Heritage|qq=|1=JSCE Civil Engineering Heritage Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423672}}. It served as the zero level reference for the Edo River water gauge and is one of the oldest level reference stones in Japan, alongside the Iinuma Level Reference Stone in [[:en:Chōshi|Chōshi]].{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.ktr.mlit.go.jp/edogawa/edogawa00229.html|title=Horie Level Reference Stone|publisher=Edo River Office|accessdate=2022-12-19}} == Auxiliary Shrines == The grounds contain the following subordinate shrines: * [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] * {{ill|Ryūjin Shrine|ja|龍神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūjin Shrine|qq=|1=Ryūjin Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11679331}} * [[:en:Dōsojin|Dōsojin]] Shrine * [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]] == festivals == [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 02.jpg|thumb|Summer purification ritual (chinowa kuguri)]] ; Annual Events * January 1: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} * February 3: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分祭|Setsubun Festival=Setsubun Festival|lt=Setsubun Festival|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分}} * February 11: Foundation Day Festival * February 17: [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] * June 15: festival ** Every four years, the grand festival (例大祭) is celebrated jointly by Seiryū Shrine, [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]], and {{ill|Toyouke Shrine|ja|豊受神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Shrine|qq=}}.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/urayasusannjya/ Urayasu Three Shrines] * June 30: {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|ja|夏越大祓|lt=Nagoshi-no-Ōharae|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae-shiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓}} * November 23: [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] * December 31: Year-end Ōharae ; Monthly Festivals * 1st and 15th of each month: Monthly Festival (Tsukinami-sai) == Related Items == * The submarine [[:en:JS_Seiryū|JS Seiryū]] distributes amulets blessed at the shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Seiryū-class submarine|ja|清瀧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū-class submarine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 清滝|1=Seiryū Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561300}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Seiryu jinja (urayasu)}} * [http://www.3ja.info/seiryujinja/ Urayasu Three Shrines - Seiryū Shrine] * [http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/kanko/716/ Chiba Prefecture Tourism - Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|清瀧神社 (浦安市)|version=104121126|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:清瀧神社]] [[Category:Municipal History of Urayasu]] {{Commons category|Seiryu-jinja (Urayasu)}} [[ja:清瀧神社 (浦安市)]] [[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q17228916}}

Monthly Festivals * 1st and 15th of each month: Monthly Festival (Tsukinami-sai) == Related Items == * The submarine [[:en:JS_Seiryū|JS Seiryū]] distributes amulets blessed at the shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Seiryū-class submarine|ja|清瀧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū-class submarine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 清滝|1=Seiryū Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561300}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Seiryu jinja (urayasu)}} * [http://www.3ja.info/seiryujinja/ Urayasu Three Shrines - Seiryū Shrine] * [http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/kanko/716/ Chiba Prefecture Tourism - Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|清瀧神社 (浦安市)|version=104121126|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:清瀧神社]] [[Category:Municipal History of Urayasu]] {{Commons category|Seiryu-jinja (Urayasu)}} [[ja:清瀧神社 (浦安市)]] {{wikidata link|Q17228916}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Seiryu-jinja (Urayasu)]][[ja:清瀧神社 (浦安市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11561325]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:19, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Seiryū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Seiryu jinja urayasu haiden.JPG|250px|Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|39|43.8|N|139|53|28.2|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], [[:en:Urayasu|Urayasu]], Horie 4-1-5 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|43.8|N|139|53|28.2|E|scale:5000}} | deity = {{ill|Ōwatatsumi|ja|大綿津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwatatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ|1=Watatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1474187}} | type = | consecration_year = [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 7 (1196) | Main Hall = | Alternate name = | festival = June 15 | Rituals = }} [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 01.jpg|thumb|230px|The first torii, rebuilt in June 2012]] '''Seiryū Shrine''' (清瀧神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Urayasu|Urayasu]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], in the Horie district. == Overview == The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|qq=}} of the shrine is [[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]], the god of the sea. The main hall, constructed in [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 2 (1855), uses wood from large zelkova trees from the [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]] and is designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. The architectural style is wooden, three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|nagare-zukuri]], and the shrine crest is a right-facing mitsudomoe (triple swirl). == History == * [[:en:1196|1196]] (Kenkyū 7): The shrine was founded according to tradition. * [[:en:1299|1299]] (Einin 1): The main hall was destroyed by a tsunami. ** The shrine also suffered tsunami damage during the Genroku, Kyōhō, and Kansei eras. * 1855 (Ansei 2): The main hall was rebuilt, and guardian lion-dog statues (komainu) were donated. * 1896 (Meiji 29): The worship hall (haiden) was constructed. * 1923 (Taishō 12): The worship hall was renovated. * 1971 (Shōwa 46): A shrine office and stone fence (tamagaki) were constructed. * 1982 (Shōwa 57): The main hall was designated a tangible cultural property by Urayasu City. * 2012 (Heisei 24): The first [[:en:Torii|torii]] was rebuilt.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/seiryuujinnjya/seibi-s-1.html Seiryū Shrine First Torii Rebuilding Ceremony, June 2, 2012] (Seiryū Shrine Official Website) == Grounds == * Main Hall - Designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Horie Level Reference Stone - Designated as a {{ill|Civil Engineering Heritage|ja|土木学会選奨土木遺産|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Engineering Heritage|1=JSCE Civil Engineering Heritage Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423672}}. It served as the zero level reference for the Edo River water gauge and is one of the oldest level reference stones in Japan, alongside the Iinuma Level Reference Stone in [[:en:Chōshi|Chōshi]].{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.ktr.mlit.go.jp/edogawa/edogawa00229.html|title=Horie Level Reference Stone|publisher=Edo River Office|accessdate=2022-12-19}} == Auxiliary Shrines == The grounds contain the following subordinate shrines: * [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] * {{ill|Ryūjin Shrine|ja|龍神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūjin Shrine|1=Ryūjin Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11679331}} * [[:en:Dōsojin|Dōsojin]] Shrine * [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]] == festivals == [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 02.jpg|thumb|Summer purification ritual (chinowa kuguri)]] ; Annual Events * January 1: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} * February 3: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分祭|Setsubun Festival=Setsubun Festival|lt=Setsubun Festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分}} * February 11: Foundation Day Festival * February 17: [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] * June 15: festival ** Every four years, the grand festival (例大祭) is celebrated jointly by Seiryū Shrine, [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]], and {{ill|Toyouke Shrine|ja|豊受神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Shrine|qq=}}.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/urayasusannjya/ Urayasu Three Shrines] * June 30: {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|ja|夏越大祓|lt=Nagoshi-no-Ōharae|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae-shiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓}} * November 23: [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] * December 31: Year-end Ōharae ; Monthly Festivals * 1st and 15th of each month: Monthly Festival (Tsukinami-sai) == Related Items == * The submarine [[:en:JS_Seiryū|JS Seiryū]] distributes amulets blessed at the shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Seiryū-class submarine|ja|清瀧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū-class submarine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 清滝|1=Seiryū Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561300}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Seiryu jinja (urayasu)}} * [http://www.3ja.info/seiryujinja/ Urayasu Three Shrines - Seiryū Shrine] * [http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/kanko/716/ Chiba Prefecture Tourism - Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|清瀧神社 (浦安市)|version=104121126|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:清瀧神社]] [[Category:Municipal History of Urayasu]] {{Commons category|Seiryu-jinja (Urayasu)}} [[ja:清瀧神社 (浦安市)]] {{wikidata link|Q17228916}}

Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|39|43.8|N|139|53|28.2|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], [[:en:Urayasu|Urayasu]], Horie 4-1-5 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|43.8|N|139|53|28.2|E|scale:5000}} | deity = {{ill|Ōwatatsumi|ja|大綿津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwatatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ|1=Watatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1474187}} | type = | consecration_year = [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 7 (1196) | Main Hall = | Alternate name = | festival = June 15 | Rituals = }} [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 01.jpg|thumb|230px|The first torii, rebuilt in June 2012]] '''Seiryū Shrine''' (清瀧神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Urayasu|Urayasu]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], in the Horie district. == Overview == The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|qq=}} of the shrine is [[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]], the god of the sea. The main hall, constructed in [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 2 (1855), uses wood from large zelkova trees from the [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]] and is designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. The architectural style is wooden, three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|nagare-zukuri]], and the shrine crest is a right-facing mitsudomoe (triple swirl). == History == * [[:en:1196|1196]] (Kenkyū 7): The shrine was founded according to tradition. * [[:en:1299|1299]] (Einin 1): The main hall was destroyed by a tsunami. ** The shrine also suffered tsunami damage during the Genroku, Kyōhō, and Kansei eras. * 1855 (Ansei 2): The main hall was rebuilt, and guardian lion-dog statues (komainu) were donated. * 1896 (Meiji 29): The worship hall (haiden) was constructed. * 1923 (Taishō 12): The worship hall was renovated. * 1971 (Shōwa 46): A shrine office and stone fence (tamagaki) were constructed. * 1982 (Shōwa 57): The main hall was designated a tangible cultural property by Urayasu City. * 2012 (Heisei 24): The first [[:en:Torii|torii]] was rebuilt.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/seiryuujinnjya/seibi-s-1.html Seiryū Shrine First Torii Rebuilding Ceremony, June 2, 2012] (Seiryū Shrine Official Website) == Grounds == * Main Hall - Designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Horie Level Reference Stone - Designated as a {{ill|Civil Engineering Heritage|ja|土木学会選奨土木遺産|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Engineering Heritage|1=JSCE Civil Engineering Heritage Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423672}}. It served as the zero level reference for the Edo River water gauge and is one of the oldest level reference stones in Japan, alongside the Iinuma Level Reference Stone in [[:en:Chōshi|Chōshi]].{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.ktr.mlit.go.jp/edogawa/edogawa00229.html|title=Horie Level Reference Stone|publisher=Edo River Office|accessdate=2022-12-19}} == Auxiliary Shrines == The grounds contain the following subordinate shrines: * [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] * {{ill|Ryūjin Shrine|ja|龍神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūjin Shrine|1=Ryūjin Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11679331}} * [[:en:Dōsojin|Dōsojin]] Shrine * [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]] == festivals == [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 02.jpg|thumb|Summer purification ritual (chinowa kuguri)]] ; Annual Events * January 1: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} * February 3: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分祭|Setsubun Festival=Setsubun Festival|lt=Setsubun Festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分}} * February 11: Foundation Day Festival * February 17: [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] * June 15: festival ** Every four years, the grand festival (例大祭) is celebrated jointly by Seiryū Shrine, [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]], and {{ill|Toyouke Shrine|ja|豊受神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Shrine|qq=}}.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/urayasusannjya/ Urayasu Three Shrines] * June 30: {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|ja|夏越大祓|lt=Nagoshi-no-Ōharae|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae-shiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓}} * November 23: [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] * December 31: Year-end Ōharae ; The main hall, constructed in [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 2 (1855), uses wood from large zelkova trees from the [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]] and is designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. The architectural style is wooden, three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|nagare-zukuri]], and the shrine crest is a right-facing mitsudomoe (triple swirl). == History == * [[:en:1196|1196]] (Kenkyū 7): The shrine was founded according to tradition. * [[:en:1299|1299]] (Einin 1): The main hall was destroyed by a tsunami. ** The shrine also suffered tsunami damage during the Genroku, Kyōhō, and Kansei eras. * 1855 (Ansei 2): The main hall was rebuilt, and guardian lion-dog statues (komainu) were donated. * 1896 (Meiji 29): The worship hall (haiden) was constructed. * 1923 (Taishō 12): The worship hall was renovated. * 1971 (Shōwa 46): A shrine office and stone fence (tamagaki) were constructed. * 1982 (Shōwa 57): The main hall was designated a tangible cultural property by Urayasu City. * 2012 (Heisei 24): The first [[:en:Torii|torii]] was rebuilt.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/seiryuujinnjya/seibi-s-1.html Seiryū Shrine First Torii Rebuilding Ceremony, June 2, 2012] (Seiryū Shrine Official Website) == Grounds == * Main Hall - Designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Horie Level Reference Stone - Designated as a {{ill|Civil Engineering Heritage|ja|土木学会選奨土木遺産|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Engineering Heritage|1=JSCE Civil Engineering Heritage Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423672}}. It served as the zero level reference for the Edo River water gauge and is one of the oldest level reference stones in Japan, alongside the Iinuma Level Reference Stone in [[:en:Chōshi|Chōshi]].{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.ktr.mlit.go.jp/edogawa/edogawa00229.html|title=Horie Level Reference Stone|publisher=Edo River Office|accessdate=2022-12-19}} == Auxiliary Shrines == The grounds contain the following subordinate shrines: * [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] * {{ill|Ryūjin Shrine|ja|龍神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūjin Shrine|1=Ryūjin Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11679331}} * [[:en:Dōsojin|Dōsojin]] Shrine * [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]] == festivals == [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 02.jpg|thumb|Summer purification ritual (chinowa kuguri)]] ; Annual Events * January 1: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} * February 3: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分祭|Setsubun Festival=Setsubun Festival|lt=Setsubun Festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分}} * February 11: Foundation Day Festival * February 17: [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] * June 15: festival ** Every four years, the grand festival (例大祭) is celebrated jointly by Seiryū Shrine, [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]], and {{ill|Toyouke Shrine|ja|豊受神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Shrine|qq=}}.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/urayasusannjya/ Urayasu Three Shrines] * June 30: {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|ja|夏越大祓|lt=Nagoshi-no-Ōharae|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae-shiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓}} * November 23: [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] * December 31: Year-end Ōharae ; Monthly Festivals * 1st and 15th of each month: Monthly Festival (Tsukinami-sai) == Related Items == * The submarine [[:en:JS_Seiryū|JS Seiryū]] distributes amulets blessed at the shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Seiryū-class submarine|ja|清瀧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū-class submarine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 清滝|1=Seiryū Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561300}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Seiryu jinja (urayasu)}} * [http://www.3ja.info/seiryujinja/ Urayasu Three Shrines - Seiryū Shrine] * [http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/kanko/716/ Chiba Prefecture Tourism - Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|清瀧神社 (浦安市)|version=104121126|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:清瀧神社]] [[Category:Municipal History of Urayasu]] {{Commons category|Seiryu-jinja (Urayasu)}} [[ja:清瀧神社 (浦安市)]] {{wikidata link|Q17228916}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

It served as the zero level reference for the Edo River water gauge and is one of the oldest level reference stones in Japan, alongside the Iinuma Level Reference Stone in [[:en:Chōshi|Chōshi]].{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.ktr.mlit.go.jp/edogawa/edogawa00229.html|title=Horie Level Reference Stone|publisher=Edo River Office|accessdate=2022-12-19}} == Auxiliary Shrines == The grounds contain the following subordinate shrines: * [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] * {{ill|Ryūjin Shrine|ja|龍神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūjin Shrine|1=Ryūjin Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11679331}} * [[:en:Dōsojin|Dōsojin]] Shrine * [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]] == festivals == [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 02.jpg|thumb|Summer purification ritual (chinowa kuguri)]] ; Annual Events * January 1: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} * February 3: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分祭|Setsubun Festival=Setsubun Festival|lt=Setsubun Festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分}} * February 11: Foundation Day Festival * February 17: [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] * June 15: festival ** Every four years, the grand festival (例大祭) is celebrated jointly by Seiryū Shrine, [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]], and {{ill|Toyouke Shrine|ja|豊受神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Shrine|qq=}}.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/urayasusannjya/ Urayasu Three Shrines] * June 30: {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|ja|夏越大祓|lt=Nagoshi-no-Ōharae|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae-shiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓}} * November 23: [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] * December 31: Year-end Ōharae ; It served as the zero level reference for the Edo River water gauge and is one of the oldest level reference stones in Japan, alongside the Iinuma Level Reference Stone in [[:en:Chōshi|Chōshi]].{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.ktr.mlit.go.jp/edogawa/edogawa00229.html|title=Horie Level Reference Stone|publisher=Edo River Office|accessdate=2022-12-19}} == Auxiliary Shrines == The grounds contain the following subordinate shrines: * [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] * {{ill|Ryūjin Shrine|ja|龍神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūjin Shrine|1=Ryūjin Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11679331}} * [[:en:Dōsojin|Dōsojin]] Shrine * [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]] == festivals == [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 02.jpg|thumb|Summer purification ritual (chinowa kuguri)]] ; Annual Events * January 1: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} * February 3: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分祭|Setsubun Festival=Setsubun Festival|lt=Setsubun Festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分}} * February 11: Foundation Day Festival * February 17: [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] * June 15: festival ** Every four years, the grand festival (例大祭) is celebrated jointly by Seiryū Shrine, [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]], and {{ill|Toyouke Shrine|ja|豊受神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Shrine|qq=}}.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/urayasusannjya/ Urayasu Three Shrines] * June 30: {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|ja|夏越大祓|lt=Nagoshi-no-Ōharae|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae-shiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓}} * November 23: [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] * December 31: Year-end Ōharae ; Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|39|43.8|N|139|53|28.2|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], [[:en:Urayasu|Urayasu]], Horie 4-1-5 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|43.8|N|139|53|28.2|E|scale:5000}} | deity = {{ill|Ōwatatsumi|ja|大綿津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwatatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ|1=Watatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1474187}} | type = | consecration_year = [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 7 (1196) | Main Hall = | Alternate name = | festival = June 15 | Rituals = }} [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 01.jpg|thumb|230px|The first torii, rebuilt in June 2012]] '''Seiryū Shrine''' (清瀧神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Urayasu|Urayasu]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], in the Horie district. == Overview == The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|qq=}} of the shrine is [[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]], the god of the sea. The main hall, constructed in [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 2 (1855), uses wood from large zelkova trees from the [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]] and is designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. The architectural style is wooden, three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|nagare-zukuri]], and the shrine crest is a right-facing mitsudomoe (triple swirl). == History == * [[:en:1196|1196]] (Kenkyū 7): The shrine was founded according to tradition. * [[:en:1299|1299]] (Einin 1): The main hall was destroyed by a tsunami. ** The shrine also suffered tsunami damage during the Genroku, Kyōhō, and Kansei eras. * 1855 (Ansei 2): The main hall was rebuilt, and guardian lion-dog statues (komainu) were donated. * 1896 (Meiji 29): The worship hall (haiden) was constructed. * 1923 (Taishō 12): The worship hall was renovated. * 1971 (Shōwa 46): A shrine office and stone fence (tamagaki) were constructed. * 1982 (Shōwa 57): The main hall was designated a tangible cultural property by Urayasu City. * 2012 (Heisei 24): The first [[:en:Torii|torii]] was rebuilt.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/seiryuujinnjya/seibi-s-1.html Seiryū Shrine First Torii Rebuilding Ceremony, June 2, 2012] (Seiryū Shrine Official Website) == Grounds == * Main Hall - Designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Horie Level Reference Stone - Designated as a {{ill|Civil Engineering Heritage|ja|土木学会選奨土木遺産|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Engineering Heritage|1=JSCE Civil Engineering Heritage Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423672}}. It served as the zero level reference for the Edo River water gauge and is one of the oldest level reference stones in Japan, alongside the Iinuma Level Reference Stone in [[:en:Chōshi|Chōshi]].{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.ktr.mlit.go.jp/edogawa/edogawa00229.html|title=Horie Level Reference Stone|publisher=Edo River Office|accessdate=2022-12-19}} == Auxiliary Shrines == The grounds contain the following subordinate shrines: * [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] * {{ill|Ryūjin Shrine|ja|龍神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūjin Shrine|1=Ryūjin Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11679331}} * [[:en:Dōsojin|Dōsojin]] Shrine * [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]] == festivals == [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 02.jpg|thumb|Summer purification ritual (chinowa kuguri)]] ; Annual Events * January 1: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} * February 3: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分祭|Setsubun Festival=Setsubun Festival|lt=Setsubun Festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分}} * February 11: Foundation Day Festival * February 17: [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] * June 15: festival ** Every four years, the grand festival (例大祭) is celebrated jointly by Seiryū Shrine, [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]], and {{ill|Toyouke Shrine|ja|豊受神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Shrine|qq=}}.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/urayasusannjya/ Urayasu Three Shrines] * June 30: {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|ja|夏越大祓|lt=Nagoshi-no-Ōharae|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae-shiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓}} * November 23: [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] * December 31: Year-end Ōharae ; Monthly Festivals * 1st and 15th of each month: Monthly Festival (Tsukinami-sai) == Related Items == * The submarine [[:en:JS_Seiryū|JS Seiryū]] distributes amulets blessed at the shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Seiryū-class submarine|ja|清瀧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū-class submarine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 清滝|1=Seiryū Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561300}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Seiryu jinja (urayasu)}} * [http://www.3ja.info/seiryujinja/ Urayasu Three Shrines - Seiryū Shrine] * [http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/kanko/716/ Chiba Prefecture Tourism - Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|清瀧神社 (浦安市)|version=104121126|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:清瀧神社]] {{Commons category|Seiryu-jinja (Urayasu)}} [[ja:清瀧神社 (浦安市)]] {{wikidata link|Q17228916}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Municipal History of Urayasu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Monthly Festivals * 1st and 15th of each month: Monthly Festival (Tsukinami-sai) == Related Items == * The submarine [[:en:JS_Seiryū|JS Seiryū]] distributes amulets blessed at the shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Seiryū-class submarine|ja|清瀧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū-class submarine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 清滝|1=Seiryū Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561300}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Seiryu jinja (urayasu)}} * [http://www.3ja.info/seiryujinja/ Urayasu Three Shrines - Seiryū Shrine] * [http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/kanko/716/ Chiba Prefecture Tourism - Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|清瀧神社 (浦安市)|version=104121126|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:清瀧神社]] {{Commons category|Seiryu-jinja (Urayasu)}} [[ja:清瀧神社 (浦安市)]] {{wikidata link|Q17228916}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Municipal History of Urayasu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[commons:Category:Seiryu-jinja (Urayasu)]][[ja:清瀧神社 (浦安市)]] [[commons:Category:Seiryu-jinja (Urayasu)]][[ja:清瀧神社 (浦安市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Seiryu-jinja (Urayasu)]][[ja:清瀧神社 (浦安市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11561325]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:19, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Seiryū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Seiryu jinja urayasu haiden.JPG|250px|Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|39|43.8|N|139|53|28.2|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], [[:en:Urayasu|Urayasu]], Horie 4-1-5 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|43.8|N|139|53|28.2|E|scale:5000}} | deity = {{ill|Ōwatatsumi|ja|大綿津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwatatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ|1=Watatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1474187}} | type = | consecration_year = [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 7 (1196) | Main Hall = | Alternate name = | festival = June 15 | Rituals = }} [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 01.jpg|thumb|230px|The first torii, rebuilt in June 2012]] '''Seiryū Shrine''' (清瀧神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Urayasu|Urayasu]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], in the Horie district. == Overview == The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|qq=}} of the shrine is [[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]], the god of the sea. The main hall, constructed in [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 2 (1855), uses wood from large zelkova trees from the [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]] and is designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. The architectural style is wooden, three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|nagare-zukuri]], and the shrine crest is a right-facing mitsudomoe (triple swirl). == History == * [[:en:1196|1196]] (Kenkyū 7): The shrine was founded according to tradition. * [[:en:1299|1299]] (Einin 1): The main hall was destroyed by a tsunami. ** The shrine also suffered tsunami damage during the Genroku, Kyōhō, and Kansei eras. * 1855 (Ansei 2): The main hall was rebuilt, and guardian lion-dog statues (komainu) were donated. * 1896 (Meiji 29): The worship hall (haiden) was constructed. * 1923 (Taishō 12): The worship hall was renovated. * 1971 (Shōwa 46): A shrine office and stone fence (tamagaki) were constructed. * 1982 (Shōwa 57): The main hall was designated a tangible cultural property by Urayasu City. * 2012 (Heisei 24): The first [[:en:Torii|torii]] was rebuilt.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/seiryuujinnjya/seibi-s-1.html Seiryū Shrine First Torii Rebuilding Ceremony, June 2, 2012] (Seiryū Shrine Official Website) == Grounds == * Main Hall - Designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Horie Level Reference Stone - Designated as a {{ill|Civil Engineering Heritage|ja|土木学会選奨土木遺産|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Engineering Heritage|1=JSCE Civil Engineering Heritage Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423672}}. It served as the zero level reference for the Edo River water gauge and is one of the oldest level reference stones in Japan, alongside the Iinuma Level Reference Stone in [[:en:Chōshi|Chōshi]].{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.ktr.mlit.go.jp/edogawa/edogawa00229.html|title=Horie Level Reference Stone|publisher=Edo River Office|accessdate=2022-12-19}} == Auxiliary Shrines == The grounds contain the following subordinate shrines: * [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] * {{ill|Ryūjin Shrine|ja|龍神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūjin Shrine|1=Ryūjin Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11679331}} * [[:en:Dōsojin|Dōsojin]] Shrine * [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]] == festivals == [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 02.jpg|thumb|Summer purification ritual (chinowa kuguri)]] ; Annual Events * January 1: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} * February 3: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分祭|Setsubun Festival=Setsubun Festival|lt=Setsubun Festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分}} * February 11: Foundation Day Festival * February 17: [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] * June 15: festival ** Every four years, the grand festival (例大祭) is celebrated jointly by Seiryū Shrine, [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]], and {{ill|Toyouke Shrine|ja|豊受神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Shrine|qq=}}.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/urayasusannjya/ Urayasu Three Shrines] * June 30: {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|ja|夏越大祓|lt=Nagoshi-no-Ōharae|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae-shiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓}} * November 23: [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] * December 31: Year-end Ōharae ; Monthly Festivals * 1st and 15th of each month: Monthly Festival (Tsukinami-sai) == Related Items == * The submarine [[:en:JS_Seiryū|JS Seiryū]] distributes amulets blessed at the shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Seiryū-class submarine|ja|清瀧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū-class submarine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 清滝|1=Seiryū Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561300}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Seiryu jinja (urayasu)}} * [http://www.3ja.info/seiryujinja/ Urayasu Three Shrines - Seiryū Shrine] * [http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/kanko/716/ Chiba Prefecture Tourism - Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|清瀧神社 (浦安市)|version=104121126|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:清瀧神社]] {{Commons category|Seiryu-jinja (Urayasu)}} [[ja:清瀧神社 (浦安市)]] {{wikidata link|Q17228916}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Municipal History of Urayasu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The main hall, constructed in [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 2 (1855), uses wood from large zelkova trees from the [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]] and is designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. The main hall, constructed in {{ill|Ansei|en|Ansei|WD=Q1327259|lt=Ansei}} 2 (1855), uses wood from large zelkova trees from the {{ill|Kazusa Province|en|Kazusa Province|WD=Q1055394|lt=Kazusa Province}} and is designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. The architectural style is wooden, three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=nagare-zukuri}}, and the shrine crest is a right-facing mitsudomoe (triple swirl). == History == * {{ill|1196|en|1196|WD=Q19747|lt=1196}} (Kenkyū 7): The shrine was founded according to tradition. * {{ill|1299|en|1299|WD=Q5564|lt=1299}} (Einin 1): The main hall was destroyed by a tsunami. ** The shrine also suffered tsunami damage during the Genroku, Kyōhō, and Kansei eras. * 1855 (Ansei 2): The main hall was rebuilt, and guardian lion-dog statues (komainu) were donated. * 1896 (Meiji 29): The worship hall (haiden) was constructed. * 1923 (Taishō 12): The worship hall was renovated. * 1971 (Shōwa 46): A shrine office and stone fence (tamagaki) were constructed. * 1982 (Shōwa 57): The main hall was designated a tangible cultural property by Urayasu City. * 2012 (Heisei 24): The first {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=torii}} was rebuilt.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/seiryuujinnjya/seibi-s-1.html Seiryū Shrine First Torii Rebuilding Ceremony, June 2, 2012] (Seiryū Shrine Official Website) == Grounds == * Main Hall - Designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Horie Level Reference Stone - Designated as a {{ill|Civil Engineering Heritage|ja|土木学会選奨土木遺産|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Engineering Heritage|1=JSCE Civil Engineering Heritage Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423672}}. It served as the zero level reference for the Edo River water gauge and is one of the oldest level reference stones in Japan, alongside the Iinuma Level Reference Stone in {{ill|Chōshi|en|Chōshi|WD=Q694317|lt=Chōshi}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.ktr.mlit.go.jp/edogawa/edogawa00229.html|title=Horie Level Reference Stone|publisher=Edo River Office|accessdate=2022-12-19}} == Auxiliary Shrines == The grounds contain the following subordinate shrines: * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|WD=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine}} * {{ill|Ryūjin Shrine|ja|龍神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūjin Shrine|1=Ryūjin Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11679331}} * {{ill|Dōsojin|en|Dōsojin|WD=Q269515|lt=Dōsojin}} Shrine * {{ill|Sengen shrine|en|Sengen shrine|WD=Q3471785|lt=Asama Shrine}} == festivals == [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 02.jpg|thumb|Summer purification ritual (chinowa kuguri)]] ; Annual Events * January 1: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} * February 3: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分祭|Setsubun Festival=Setsubun Festival|lt=Setsubun Festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分}} * February 11: Foundation Day Festival * February 17: {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai}} * June 15: festival ** Every four years, the grand festival (例大祭) is celebrated jointly by Seiryū Shrine, {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine}}, and {{ill|Toyouke Shrine|ja|豊受神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Shrine|qq=}}.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/urayasusannjya/ Urayasu Three Shrines] * June 30: {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|ja|夏越大祓|lt=Nagoshi-no-Ōharae|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae-shiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓}} * November 23: {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai}} * December 31: Year-end Ōharae ; Monthly Festivals * 1st and 15th of each month: Monthly Festival (Tsukinami-sai) == Related Items == * The submarine {{ill|JS_Seiryū|en|JS_Seiryū|WD=Q27983110|lt=JS Seiryū}} distributes amulets blessed at the shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Seiryū-class submarine|ja|清瀧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū-class submarine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 清滝|1=Seiryū Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561300}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Seiryu jinja (urayasu)}} * [http://www.3ja.info/seiryujinja/ Urayasu Three Shrines - Seiryū Shrine] * [http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/kanko/716/ Chiba Prefecture Tourism - Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|清瀧神社 (浦安市)|version=104121126|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:清瀧神社]] {{Commons category|Seiryu-jinja (Urayasu)}} [[ja:清瀧神社 (浦安市)]] {{wikidata link|Q17228916}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Municipal History of Urayasu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Seiryu-jinja (Urayasu)]][[ja:清瀧神社 (浦安市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Seiryu-jinja (Urayasu)]][[ja:清瀧神社 (浦安市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Seiryu-jinja (Urayasu)]][[ja:清瀧神社 (浦安市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11561325]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:19, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Seiryū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Seiryu jinja urayasu haiden.JPG|250px|Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|39|43.8|N|139|53|28.2|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}, {{ill|Urayasu|en|Urayasu|WD=Q319729|lt=Urayasu}}, Horie 4-1-5 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|43.8|N|139|53|28.2|E|scale:5000}} | deity = {{ill|Ōwatatsumi|ja|大綿津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwatatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ|1=Watatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1474187}} | type = | consecration_year = {{ill|Kenkyū|en|Kenkyū|WD=Q1193933|lt=Kenkyū}} 7 (1196) | Main Hall = | Alternate name = | festival = June 15 | Rituals = }} [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 01.jpg|thumb|230px|The first torii, rebuilt in June 2012]] '''Seiryū Shrine''' (清瀧神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Urayasu|en|Urayasu|WD=Q319729|lt=Urayasu}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}, in the Horie district. == Overview == The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|qq=}} of the shrine is {{ill|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|WD=Q1474187|lt=Watatsumi}}, the god of the sea. The main hall, constructed in {{ill|Ansei|en|Ansei|WD=Q1327259|lt=Ansei}} 2 (1855), uses wood from large zelkova trees from the {{ill|Kazusa Province|en|Kazusa Province|WD=Q1055394|lt=Kazusa Province}} and is designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. The architectural style is wooden, three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=nagare-zukuri}}, and the shrine crest is a right-facing mitsudomoe (triple swirl). == History == * {{ill|1196|en|1196|WD=Q19747|lt=1196}} (Kenkyū 7): The shrine was founded according to tradition. * {{ill|1299|en|1299|WD=Q5564|lt=1299}} (Einin 1): The main hall was destroyed by a tsunami. ** The shrine also suffered tsunami damage during the Genroku, Kyōhō, and Kansei eras. * 1855 (Ansei 2): The main hall was rebuilt, and guardian lion-dog statues (komainu) were donated. * 1896 (Meiji 29): The worship hall (haiden) was constructed. * 1923 (Taishō 12): The worship hall was renovated. * 1971 (Shōwa 46): A shrine office and stone fence (tamagaki) were constructed. * 1982 (Shōwa 57): The main hall was designated a tangible cultural property by Urayasu City. * 2012 (Heisei 24): The first {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=torii}} was rebuilt.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/seiryuujinnjya/seibi-s-1.html Seiryū Shrine First Torii Rebuilding Ceremony, June 2, 2012] (Seiryū Shrine Official Website) == Grounds == * Main Hall - Designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Horie Level Reference Stone - Designated as a {{ill|Civil Engineering Heritage|ja|土木学会選奨土木遺産|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Engineering Heritage|1=JSCE Civil Engineering Heritage Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423672}}. It served as the zero level reference for the Edo River water gauge and is one of the oldest level reference stones in Japan, alongside the Iinuma Level Reference Stone in {{ill|Chōshi|en|Chōshi|WD=Q694317|lt=Chōshi}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.ktr.mlit.go.jp/edogawa/edogawa00229.html|title=Horie Level Reference Stone|publisher=Edo River Office|accessdate=2022-12-19}} == Auxiliary Shrines == The grounds contain the following subordinate shrines: * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|WD=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine}} * {{ill|Ryūjin Shrine|ja|龍神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūjin Shrine|1=Ryūjin Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11679331}} * {{ill|Dōsojin|en|Dōsojin|WD=Q269515|lt=Dōsojin}} Shrine * {{ill|Sengen shrine|en|Sengen shrine|WD=Q3471785|lt=Asama Shrine}} == festivals == [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 02.jpg|thumb|Summer purification ritual (chinowa kuguri)]] ; Annual Events * January 1: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} * February 3: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分祭|Setsubun Festival=Setsubun Festival|lt=Setsubun Festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分}} * February 11: Foundation Day Festival * February 17: {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai}} * June 15: festival ** Every four years, the grand festival (例大祭) is celebrated jointly by Seiryū Shrine, {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine}}, and {{ill|Toyouke Shrine|ja|豊受神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Shrine|qq=}}.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/urayasusannjya/ Urayasu Three Shrines] * June 30: {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|ja|夏越大祓|lt=Nagoshi-no-Ōharae|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae-shiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓}} * November 23: {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai}} * December 31: Year-end Ōharae ; Monthly Festivals * 1st and 15th of each month: Monthly Festival (Tsukinami-sai) == Related Items == * The submarine {{ill|JS_Seiryū|en|JS_Seiryū|WD=Q27983110|lt=JS Seiryū}} distributes amulets blessed at the shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Seiryū-class submarine|ja|清瀧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū-class submarine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 清滝|1=Seiryū Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561300}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Seiryu jinja (urayasu)}} * [http://www.3ja.info/seiryujinja/ Urayasu Three Shrines - Seiryū Shrine] * [http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/kanko/716/ Chiba Prefecture Tourism - Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|清瀧神社 (浦安市)|version=104121126|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:清瀧神社]] {{Commons category|Seiryu-jinja (Urayasu)}} [[ja:清瀧神社 (浦安市)]] {{wikidata link|Q17228916}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Municipal History of Urayasu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|39|43.8|N|139|53|28.2|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}, {{ill|Urayasu|en|Urayasu|WD=Q319729|lt=Urayasu}}, Horie 4-1-5 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|43.8|N|139|53|28.2|E|scale:5000}} | deity = {{ill|Ōwatatsumi|ja|大綿津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwatatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ|1=Watatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1474187}} | type = | consecration_year = {{ill|Kenkyū|en|Kenkyū|WD=Q1193933|lt=Kenkyū}} 7 (1196) | Main Hall = | Alternate name = | festival = June 15 | Rituals = }} [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 01.jpg|thumb|230px|The first torii, rebuilt in June 2012]] '''Seiryū Shrine''' (清瀧神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Urayasu|en|Urayasu|WD=Q319729|lt=Urayasu}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}, in the Horie district. == Overview == The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|qq=}} of the shrine is {{ill|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|WD=Q1474187|lt=Watatsumi}}, the god of the sea. The main hall, constructed in {{ill|Ansei|en|Ansei|WD=Q1327259|lt=Ansei}} 2 (1855), uses wood from large zelkova trees from the {{ill|Kazusa Province|en|Kazusa Province|WD=Q1055394|lt=Kazusa Province}} and is designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. The architectural style is wooden, three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=nagare-zukuri}}, and the shrine crest is a right-facing mitsudomoe (triple swirl). == History == * {{ill|1196|en|1196|WD=Q19747|lt=1196}} (Kenkyū 7): The shrine was founded according to tradition. * {{ill|1299|en|1299|WD=Q5564|lt=1299}} (Einin 1): The main hall was destroyed by a tsunami. ** The shrine also suffered tsunami damage during the Genroku, Kyōhō, and Kansei eras. * 1855 (Ansei 2): The main hall was rebuilt, and guardian lion-dog statues (komainu) were donated. * 1896 (Meiji 29): The worship hall (haiden) was constructed. * 1923 (Taishō 12): The worship hall was renovated. * 1971 (Shōwa 46): A shrine office and stone fence (tamagaki) were constructed. * 1982 (Shōwa 57): The main hall was designated a tangible cultural property by Urayasu City. * 2012 (Heisei 24): The first {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=torii}} was rebuilt.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/seiryuujinnjya/seibi-s-1.html Seiryū Shrine First Torii Rebuilding Ceremony, June 2, 2012] (Seiryū Shrine Official Website) == Grounds == * Main Hall - Designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Horie Level Reference Stone - Designated as a {{ill|Civil Engineering Heritage|ja|土木学会選奨土木遺産|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Engineering Heritage|1=JSCE Civil Engineering Heritage Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423672}}. It served as the zero level reference for the Edo River water gauge and is one of the oldest level reference stones in Japan, alongside the Iinuma Level Reference Stone in {{ill|Chōshi|en|Chōshi|WD=Q694317|lt=Chōshi}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.ktr.mlit.go.jp/edogawa/edogawa00229.html|title=Horie Level Reference Stone|publisher=Edo River Office|accessdate=2022-12-19}} == Auxiliary Shrines == The grounds contain the following subordinate shrines: * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|WD=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine}} * {{ill|Ryūjin Shrine|ja|龍神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūjin Shrine|1=Ryūjin Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11679331}} * {{ill|Dōsojin|en|Dōsojin|WD=Q269515|lt=Dōsojin}} Shrine * {{ill|Sengen shrine|en|Sengen shrine|WD=Q3471785|lt=Asama Shrine}} == festivals == [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 02.jpg|thumb|Summer purification ritual (chinowa kuguri)]] ; Annual Events * January 1: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} * February 3: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分祭|Setsubun Festival=Setsubun Festival|lt=Setsubun Festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分}} * February 11: Foundation Day Festival * February 17: {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai}} * June 15: festival ** Every four years, the grand festival (例大祭) is celebrated jointly by Seiryū Shrine, {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine}}, and {{ill|Toyouke Shrine|ja|豊受神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Shrine|qq=}}.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/urayasusannjya/ Urayasu Three Shrines] * June 30: {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|ja|夏越大祓|lt=Nagoshi-no-Ōharae|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae-shiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓}} * November 23: {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai}} * December 31: Year-end Ōharae ; Monthly Festivals * 1st and 15th of each month: Monthly Festival (Tsukinami-sai) == Related Items == * The submarine {{ill|JS_Seiryū|en|JS_Seiryū|WD=Q27983110|lt=JS Seiryū}} distributes amulets blessed at the shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Seiryū-class submarine|ja|清瀧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū-class submarine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 清滝|1=Seiryū Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561300}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Seiryu jinja (urayasu)}} * [http://www.3ja.info/seiryujinja/ Urayasu Three Shrines - Seiryū Shrine] * [http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/kanko/716/ Chiba Prefecture Tourism - Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|清瀧神社 (浦安市)|version=104121126|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:清瀧神社]] {{Commons category|Seiryu-jinja (Urayasu)}} [[ja:清瀧神社 (浦安市)]] {{wikidata link|Q17228916}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Municipal History of Urayasu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|39|43.8|N|139|53|28.2|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, {{ill|Urayasu|en|Urayasu|WD=Q319729|lt=Urayasu|1=Urayasu}}, Horie 4-1-5 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|43.8|N|139|53|28.2|E|scale:5000}} | deity = {{ill|Ōwatatsumi|ja|大綿津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwatatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ|1=Watatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1474187}} | type = | consecration_year = {{ill|Kenkyū|en|Kenkyū|WD=Q1193933|lt=Kenkyū|1=Kenkyū}} 7 (1196) | Main Hall = | Alternate name = | festival = June 15 | Rituals = }} [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 01.jpg|thumb|230px|The first torii, rebuilt in June 2012]] '''Seiryū Shrine''' (清瀧神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Urayasu|en|Urayasu|WD=Q319729|lt=Urayasu|1=Urayasu}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, in the Horie district. == Overview == The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|qq=}} of the shrine is {{ill|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|WD=Q1474187|lt=Watatsumi|1=Watatsumi}}, the god of the sea. The main hall, constructed in {{ill|Ansei|en|Ansei|WD=Q1327259|lt=Ansei|1=Ansei}} 2 (1855), uses wood from large zelkova trees from the {{ill|Kazusa Province|en|Kazusa Province|WD=Q1055394|lt=Kazusa Province|1=Kazusa Province}} and is designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. The architectural style is wooden, three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, and the shrine crest is a right-facing mitsudomoe (triple swirl). == History == * {{ill|1196|en|1196|WD=Q19747|lt=1196|1=1196}} (Kenkyū 7): The shrine was founded according to tradition. * {{ill|1299|en|1299|WD=Q5564|lt=1299|1=1299}} (Einin 1): The main hall was destroyed by a tsunami. ** The shrine also suffered tsunami damage during the Genroku, Kyōhō, and Kansei eras. * 1855 (Ansei 2): The main hall was rebuilt, and guardian lion-dog statues (komainu) were donated. * 1896 (Meiji 29): The worship hall (haiden) was constructed. * 1923 (Taishō 12): The worship hall was renovated. * 1971 (Shōwa 46): A shrine office and stone fence (tamagaki) were constructed. * 1982 (Shōwa 57): The main hall was designated a tangible cultural property by Urayasu City. * 2012 (Heisei 24): The first {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=torii|1=Torii}} was rebuilt.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/seiryuujinnjya/seibi-s-1.html Seiryū Shrine First Torii Rebuilding Ceremony, June 2, 2012] (Seiryū Shrine Official Website) == Grounds == * Main Hall - Designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Horie Level Reference Stone - Designated as a {{ill|Civil Engineering Heritage|ja|土木学会選奨土木遺産|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Engineering Heritage|1=JSCE Civil Engineering Heritage Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423672}}. It served as the zero level reference for the Edo River water gauge and is one of the oldest level reference stones in Japan, alongside the Iinuma Level Reference Stone in {{ill|Chōshi|en|Chōshi|WD=Q694317|lt=Chōshi|1=Chōshi}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.ktr.mlit.go.jp/edogawa/edogawa00229.html|title=Horie Level Reference Stone|publisher=Edo River Office|accessdate=2022-12-19}} == Auxiliary Shrines == The grounds contain the following subordinate shrines: * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|WD=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=Yasaka Shrine}} * {{ill|Ryūjin Shrine|ja|龍神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūjin Shrine|1=Ryūjin Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11679331}} * {{ill|Dōsojin|en|Dōsojin|WD=Q269515|lt=Dōsojin|1=Dōsojin}} Shrine * {{ill|Sengen shrine|en|Sengen shrine|WD=Q3471785|lt=Asama Shrine|1=Sengen shrine}} == festivals == [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 02.jpg|thumb|Summer purification ritual (chinowa kuguri)]] ; Annual Events * January 1: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} * February 3: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分祭|Setsubun Festival=Setsubun Festival|lt=Setsubun Festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分}} * February 11: Foundation Day Festival * February 17: {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai|1=Kinensai}} * June 15: festival ** Every four years, the grand festival (例大祭) is celebrated jointly by Seiryū Shrine, {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine|1=Inari shrine}}, and {{ill|Toyouke Shrine|ja|豊受神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Shrine|qq=}}.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/urayasusannjya/ Urayasu Three Shrines] * June 30: {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|ja|夏越大祓|lt=Nagoshi-no-Ōharae|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae-shiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓}} * November 23: {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} * December 31: Year-end Ōharae ; Monthly Festivals * 1st and 15th of each month: Monthly Festival (Tsukinami-sai) == Related Items == * The submarine {{ill|JS_Seiryū|en|JS_Seiryū|WD=Q27983110|lt=JS Seiryū|1=JS Seiryū}} distributes amulets blessed at the shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Seiryū-class submarine|ja|清瀧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū-class submarine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 清滝|1=Seiryū Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561300}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Seiryu jinja (urayasu)}} * [http://www.3ja.info/seiryujinja/ Urayasu Three Shrines - Seiryū Shrine] * [http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/kanko/716/ Chiba Prefecture Tourism - Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|清瀧神社 (浦安市)|version=104121126|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:清瀧神社]] {{Commons category|Seiryu-jinja (Urayasu)}} [[ja:清瀧神社 (浦安市)]] {{wikidata link|Q17228916}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Municipal History of Urayasu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Seiryu-jinja (Urayasu)]][[ja:清瀧神社 (浦安市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Seiryu-jinja (Urayasu)]][[ja:清瀧神社 (浦安市)]] [[ja:清瀧神社 (浦安市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Seiryū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Seiryu jinja urayasu haiden.JPG|250px|Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|39|43.8|N|139|53|28.2|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, {{ill|Urayasu|en|Urayasu|WD=Q319729|lt=Urayasu|1=Urayasu}}, Horie 4-1-5 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|43.8|N|139|53|28.2|E|scale:5000}} | deity = {{ill|Ōwatatsumi|ja|大綿津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwatatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ|1=Watatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1474187}} | type = | consecration_year = {{ill|Kenkyū|en|Kenkyū|WD=Q1193933|lt=Kenkyū|1=Kenkyū}} 7 (1196) | Main Hall = | Alternate name = | festival = June 15 | Rituals = }} [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 01.jpg|thumb|230px|The first torii, rebuilt in June 2012]] '''Seiryū Shrine''' (清瀧神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Urayasu|en|Urayasu|WD=Q319729|lt=Urayasu|1=Urayasu}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, in the Horie district. == Overview == The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|qq=}} of the shrine is {{ill|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|WD=Q1474187|lt=Watatsumi|1=Watatsumi}}, the god of the sea. The main hall, constructed in {{ill|Ansei|en|Ansei|WD=Q1327259|lt=Ansei|1=Ansei}} 2 (1855), uses wood from large zelkova trees from the {{ill|Kazusa Province|en|Kazusa Province|WD=Q1055394|lt=Kazusa Province|1=Kazusa Province}} and is designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. The architectural style is wooden, three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, and the shrine crest is a right-facing mitsudomoe (triple swirl). == History == * {{ill|1196|en|1196|WD=Q19747|lt=1196|1=1196}} (Kenkyū 7): The shrine was founded according to tradition. * {{ill|1299|en|1299|WD=Q5564|lt=1299|1=1299}} (Einin 1): The main hall was destroyed by a tsunami. ** The shrine also suffered tsunami damage during the Genroku, Kyōhō, and Kansei eras. * 1855 (Ansei 2): The main hall was rebuilt, and guardian lion-dog statues (komainu) were donated. * 1896 (Meiji 29): The worship hall (haiden) was constructed. * 1923 (Taishō 12): The worship hall was renovated. * 1971 (Shōwa 46): A shrine office and stone fence (tamagaki) were constructed. * 1982 (Shōwa 57): The main hall was designated a tangible cultural property by Urayasu City. * 2012 (Heisei 24): The first {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=torii|1=Torii}} was rebuilt.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/seiryuujinnjya/seibi-s-1.html Seiryū Shrine First Torii Rebuilding Ceremony, June 2, 2012] (Seiryū Shrine Official Website) == Grounds == * Main Hall - Designated as a cultural property of Urayasu City. * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Horie Level Reference Stone - Designated as a {{ill|Civil Engineering Heritage|ja|土木学会選奨土木遺産|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Engineering Heritage|1=JSCE Civil Engineering Heritage Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423672}}. It served as the zero level reference for the Edo River water gauge and is one of the oldest level reference stones in Japan, alongside the Iinuma Level Reference Stone in {{ill|Chōshi|en|Chōshi|WD=Q694317|lt=Chōshi|1=Chōshi}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.ktr.mlit.go.jp/edogawa/edogawa00229.html|title=Horie Level Reference Stone|publisher=Edo River Office|accessdate=2022-12-19}} == Auxiliary Shrines == The grounds contain the following subordinate shrines: * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|WD=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=Yasaka Shrine}} * {{ill|Ryūjin Shrine|ja|龍神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūjin Shrine|1=Ryūjin Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11679331}} * {{ill|Dōsojin|en|Dōsojin|WD=Q269515|lt=Dōsojin|1=Dōsojin}} Shrine * {{ill|Sengen shrine|en|Sengen shrine|WD=Q3471785|lt=Asama Shrine|1=Sengen shrine}} == festivals == [[File:Seiryu jinja (urayasu) 02.jpg|thumb|Summer purification ritual (chinowa kuguri)]] ; Annual Events * January 1: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} * February 3: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分祭|Setsubun Festival=Setsubun Festival|lt=Setsubun Festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分}} * February 11: Foundation Day Festival * February 17: {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai|1=Kinensai}} * June 15: festival ** Every four years, the grand festival (例大祭) is celebrated jointly by Seiryū Shrine, {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine|1=Inari shrine}}, and {{ill|Toyouke Shrine|ja|豊受神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Shrine|qq=}}.[http://members3.jcom.home.ne.jp/urayasusannjya/ Urayasu Three Shrines] * June 30: {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|ja|夏越大祓|lt=Nagoshi-no-Ōharae|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae-shiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓}} * November 23: {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} * December 31: Year-end Ōharae ; Monthly Festivals * 1st and 15th of each month: Monthly Festival (Tsukinami-sai) == Related Items == * The submarine {{ill|JS_Seiryū|en|JS_Seiryū|WD=Q27983110|lt=JS Seiryū|1=JS Seiryū}} distributes amulets blessed at the shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Seiryū-class submarine|ja|清瀧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū-class submarine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 清滝|1=Seiryū Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561300}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Seiryu jinja (urayasu)}} * [http://www.3ja.info/seiryujinja/ Urayasu Three Shrines - Seiryū Shrine] * [http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/kanko/716/ Chiba Prefecture Tourism - Seiryū Shrine (Urayasu)] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|清瀧神社 (浦安市)|version=104121126|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:清瀧神社]] {{Commons category|Seiryu-jinja (Urayasu)}} [[ja:清瀧神社 (浦安市)]] {{wikidata link|Q17228916}} [[Category:Municipal History of Urayasu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
